content.text: Hello! How can I help you today?

message.content: Let's build a static site with the contents of 2 markdown files here. /Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/justNICE.us. Then,  We're going to deploy to AWS lightsail server docker - the same way we deployed /Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/v3gifteddreamers.git by using github actions. There is no github repo yet. I created a blank repo at https://github.com/Gifted-Dreamers/justnice.us. The copyright footer should say 2026 Gifted Dreamers, Inc. Texas-based 501(c)(3) EIN 39-3863796 and include a link to gifteddreamers.org/GRUHP to donate. Please copy the donate buttons from v3gifteddreamers and include the head script and widget code. Please indicate that all donations go 100% to mutual aid. There are files for favicon and a logo file to follow for the branding colors.

1→# Immigrant Resources: Legal Aid, Mutual Aid & Emergency Preparedness
     2→
     3→> **Last Updated:** February 9, 2026
     4→> A comprehensive directory of free and low-cost legal services, mutual aid networks, bond funds, rapid response hotlines, family preparedness tools, and community support for immigrants in the United States.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## 📞 Part 1: Emergency Hotlines & Rapid Response Networks
     9→
    10→### National Hotlines
    11→
    12→| Hotline | Number | Hours / Notes |
    13→|---|---|---|
    14→| **United We Dream Hotline** | 1-844-363-1423 | National immigration emergency line |
    15→| **National Immigration Detention Hotline** (Freedom for Immigrants) | Dial **9233#** from detention facility phone | Mon–Fri, 8 AM – 8 PM PT / 11 AM – 11 PM ET — [freedomforimmigrants.org/hotline](https://www.freedomforimmigrants.org/hotline) |
    16→| **UNHCR Refugee/Asylum Hotline** | 202-461-2356 (or **#566** from detention) | For refugees and asylum seekers |
    17→| **NAKASEC (Korean/English)** | 1-844-500-3222 | 24-hour hotline |
    18→| **SPLC Southeast Immigrant Freedom Initiative (SIFI)** | 1-800-591-3656 or 1-404-521-6700 | Helpline for detained immigrants in the South |
    19→| **Tahirih Afghan Asylum Line** | 1-888-991-0852 | Mon–Fri, 10 AM – 4 PM ET |
    20→| **NIJC (If detained by ICE)** | Collect call: (312) 583-9721 or 3-digit code **565** | [immigrantjustice.org](https://immigrantjustice.org/contact-us/) |
    21→| **ABA Guantanamo Bay Hotline** | 1-855-641-6081 | Only for confirmed Guantanamo Bay detainees |
    22→| **Mexican Consular Hotline** | 1-855-463-6395 | Emergency assistance for Mexican nationals |
    23→
    24→### Regional Hotlines by City/State
    25→
    26→| Location | Hotline | Number |
    27→|---|---|---|
    28→| **New York State** | NY Immigration Hotline | 1-800-566-7636 (Mon–Fri, 8 AM – 6 PM) |
    29→| **NYC** | ActionNYC (Mayor's Office of Immigrant Affairs) | 1-800-354-0365 |
    30→| **NYC** | Legal Aid | 1-844-955-3425 |
    31→| **NYC** | Immigrant Defense Project | 212-725-6422 |
    32→| **Chicago, IL** | IL Coalition for Immigrant & Refugee Rights | 1-855-435-7693 (1-855-HELP-MY-FAMILY) |
    33→| **Houston, TX** | Houston Immigration Legal Services Collaborative | 833-HOU-IMMI |
    34→| **Houston, TX** | FIEL Houston | 1-713-364-3435 |
    35→| **Washington, DC** | DMV Immigration Crisis Hotline | 202-335-1183 |
    36→| **Baltimore/DC** | CASA Hotline | 1-855-678-2272 |
    37→| **Denver, CO** | Colorado Rapid Response Network | 1-844-864-8341 |
    38→| **Denver, CO** | Colorado Immigrant Rights Coalition (CIRC) | 303-922-3344 |
    39→| **Miami, FL** | Americans for Immigrant Justice | (305) 573-1106 |
    40→| **Florida** | ACLU FL Rapid Response Alliance | 1-888-600-5762 — [aclufl.org](https://www.aclufl.org/rapid-response-alliance-immigrant-safety-and-empowerment-hotline/) |
    41→| **Portland, OR** | Immigrants Rights Coalition | 1-888-622-1510 |
    42→| **Seattle, WA** | Raid Report Hotline | 1-844-724-3737 (6 AM – 9 PM) |
    43→| **Philadelphia, PA** | New Sanctuary Movement | 1-267-333-9530 |
    44→| **Albuquerque, NM** | Immigration Enforcement Report / Legal Help | 1-844-363-1423 ext. 102 |
    45→
    46→### California Rapid Response Network Hotlines
    47→
    48→| Area | Number |
    49→|---|---|
    50→| **California State Trust Act Hotline** | 1-844-878-7801 |
    51→| **Los Angeles** | 888-624-4752 |
    52→| **Long Beach** | 562-245-9575 |
    53→| **Orange County** | 714-881-1558 |
    54→| **San Diego** | 619-536-0823 |
    55→| **San Francisco** | 415-200-1548 |
    56→| **San Mateo County** | 203-666-4472 |
    57→| **Santa Clara County** | 408-290-1144 |
    58→| **Alameda County** | 510-241-4011 |
    59→| **Sacramento** | 916-382-0256 |
    60→| **Marin County** | 415-991-4545 |
    61→| **North Bay (Sonoma, Napa, Solano)** | 707-800-4544 |
    62→| **Humboldt County (Central del Pueblo)** | 707-200-8091 |
    63→| **Monterey County** | 831-204-8082 |
    64→| **Santa Cruz County** | 831-239-4289 |
    65→| **805 Network (Santa Barbara, Ventura, SLO)** | 805-870-8855 |
    66→| **Central Valley (Fresno, San Joaquin, Merced, Kern)** | 559-206-0151 |
    67→| **San Bernardino & Riverside Counties** | 909-361-4588 |
    68→| **SoCal Bilingual Legal Resource (if detained)** | 213-833-8283 |
    69→| **North County San Diego (Alianza Comunitaria)** | Text "Alianza" to 33733 (español) / "Alianza2" to 33733 (English) |
    70→
    71→> 📋 **Full list of Rapid Response hotlines (PDF):** [publicdefenders.us/Hotlines-RRN.pdf](https://publicdefenders.us/app/uploads/2025/01/Hotlines-RRN.doc.pdf)
    72→>
    73→> 📋 **National Network for Immigrant & Refugee Rights hotlines:** [nnirr.org/immigration-hotlines](https://nnirr.org/education-resources/community-resources-legal-assistance-recursos-comunitarios-asistencia-legal/immigration-hotlines/)
    74→
    75→---
    76→
    77→## ⚖️ Part 2: Free & Low-Cost Legal Services
    78→
    79→### National Legal Directories
    80→
    81→| Resource | Description | Link |
    82→|---|---|---|
    83→| **Immigration Advocates Network / ImmigrationLawHelp** | Searchable directory of **free/low-cost** nonprofit immigration legal services by state, zip code, detention facility, and area of law | [immigrationadvocates.org/legaldirectory](https://www.immigrationadvocates.org/legaldirectory/) |
    84→| **DOJ List of Pro Bono Legal Service Providers** | Official government list of free legal services recognized by immigration courts | [justice.gov/eoir/probono](https://www.justice.gov/eoir/list-pro-bono-legal-service-providers) |
    85→| **AILA Lawyer Directory** | American Immigration Lawyers Association directory of private immigration attorneys (fees vary; ask about free consultations) | [ailalawyer.com](https://ailalawyer.com/) |
    86→| **CLINIC Affiliate Directory** | Catholic Legal Immigration Network — locates qualified legal representatives | [cliniclegal.org](https://www.cliniclegal.org/epif) |
    87→| **LawHelp.org** | State-by-state legal aid finder | [lawhelp.org/finding-immigration-help](https://www.lawhelp.org/resource/finding-immigration-help) |
    88→| **USAHello Guide** | Step-by-step guide to finding free immigration lawyers, avoiding scams | [usahello.org/legal-help](https://usahello.org/immigration/your-rights/legal-help-for-immigrants/) |
    89→| **ILRC Legal Services Directory** | Non-profit legal services searchable by state, county, or detention facility | [ilrc.org/know-your-rights](https://www.ilrc.org/community-resources/know-your-rights) |
    90→
    91→### Key National Legal Organizations
    92→
    93→| Organization | What They Do | Contact / Link |
    94→|---|---|---|
    95→| **National Immigrant Justice Center (NIJC)** | Free legal services for detained immigrants; asylum, VAWA, trafficking cases | Call (312) 660-1370 (Tue/Wed/Thu, 8:30 AM – 12 PM CT) — [immigrantjustice.org](https://immigrantjustice.org) |
    96→| **National Immigration Law Center (NILC)** | Policy advocacy + legal resources; guide to finding loved ones after arrest | [nilc.org](https://www.nilc.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/How-to-Locate-a-ICE-Disappearance.pdf) |
    97→| **American Immigration Council** | Legal resources, policy analysis, community guides | [americanimmigrationcouncil.org](https://www.americanimmigrationcouncil.org/blog/what-to-do-if-ice-immigration-enforcement-comes-to-town/) |
    98→| **Immigrant Defense Project (IDP)** | Know Your Rights, ICE raid response, legal hotline | [immigrantdefenseproject.org](https://www.immigrantdefenseproject.org/know-your-rights../) |
    99→| **RAICES (Texas)** | Free legal services for detained immigrants; bond fund; asylum representation | [raicestexas.org](https://raicestexas.org/bond) |
   100→| **Houston Immigration Legal Services Collaborative (HILSC)** | Legal services fund for Houston-area immigrants | [houstonimmigration.org](https://houstonimmigration.org/hilsc-program/immigrant-legal-services-fund/) |
   101→| **HIAS** | Pro bono legal program, accompaniment to hearings, Welcome Circles | [hias.org](https://hias.org/how/support-our-immigrant-neighbors/) |
   102→| **Immigration Equality** | Legal resources for **LGBTQ+ and/or HIV-positive** immigrants (asylum, detention) | [immigrationequality.org](https://www.immigrationequality.org/) |
   103→| **Asylum Seeker Advocacy Project (ASAP)** | Free membership for asylum seekers — access to expert immigration attorneys | [asylumadvocacy.org](https://www.asylumadvocacy.org/) |
   104→| **Kids in Need of Defense (KIND)** | Legal services for unaccompanied immigrant children | [supportkind.org](https://supportkind.org/) |
   105→| **Citizenshipworks** | Free online legal help for the citizenship process | [citizenshipworks.org](https://www.citizenshipworks.org/) |
   106→
   107→### How to Avoid Immigration Scams
   108→
   109→From [USAHello](https://usahello.org/immigration/your-rights/legal-help-for-immigrants/):
   110→
   111→- ❌ **Notaries public (notarios)** in the U.S. are **NOT** authorized to provide legal services
   112→- ❌ Beware of anyone who contacts you first, makes guarantees, or asks you to sign blank forms
   113→- ❌ Never pay for blank immigration forms (they are free from USCIS)
   114→- ✅ Verify lawyers with the **State Bar Association** and **AILA**
   115→- ✅ Verify DOJ-accredited representatives at the [DOJ's official list](https://www.justice.gov/eoir/list-pro-bono-legal-service-providers)
   116→
   117→---
   118→
   119→## 🔓 Part 3: Immigration Bond Funds
   120→
   121→If a loved one is detained by ICE, they may be eligible for release on bond. Bond amounts have **no maximum cap** and can range from $1,500 to $80,000+. These organizations help pay bonds for people who cannot afford them.
   122→
   123→### National Bond Funds
   124→
   125→| Fund | Link |
   126→|---|---|
   127→| **National Bail Fund Network (NBFN)** — umbrella network | [communityjusticeexchange.org/nbfn-directory](https://www.communityjusticeexchange.org/en/nbfn-directory) |
   128→| **NBFN Immigration Bond Referral Program** | [communityjusticeexchange.org/immigration-directory](https://www.communityjusticeexchange.org/en/immigration-directory) |
   129→| **Community Justice Exchange** | [communityjusticeexchange.org](https://www.communityjusticeexchange.org/) |
   130→| **Envision Freedom Fund** | [envisionfreedomfund.org](https://www.envisionfreedomfund.org/) |
   131→| **The Bail Project** | [bailproject.org](https://www.bailproject.org/) |
   132→| **Black Immigrants Bail Fund** | [blackimmigrantsbailfund.org](https://www.blackimmigrantsbailfund.org/) |
   133→| **LGBTQ Freedom Fund** | [lgbtqfreedomfund.org](https://www.lgbtqfreedomfund.org/) |
   134→
   135→### State & Regional Bond Funds
   136→
   137→| State | Fund |
   138→|---|---|
   139→| **Arizona** | Bond Fund for Immigrants — (520) 884-1776 |
   140→| **California** | NorCal Resist Sacramento; Orange County Justice Fund; Long Beach Liberation Fund; Familias Reunidas Bond Program; Borderlands Get Free Fund (San Diego) |
   141→| **Colorado** | Colorado Freedom Fund; Immigrant Freedom Fund |
   142→| **Connecticut** | Connecticut Bail Fund; Immigrant Bail Fund |
   143→| **Massachusetts** | Massachusetts Bail Fund; Beyond Bond & Legal Defense Fund |
   144→| **Michigan** | Kent County Immigration Bond for Our Neighbor's Defense Fund |
   145→| **Nevada** | Las Vegas Family Unity Bond Fund (Arriba Las Vegas Workers Center) |
   146→| **New Jersey** | First Friends of NJ & NY Bond Fund |
   147→| **New York** | Envision Freedom Fund; First Friends of NJ & NY |
   148→| **Pennsylvania** | Bukit Bail Fund; Philadelphia Bail Fund; Community Fund for Bond and Legal Support |
   149→| **Texas** | **RAICES Bond Fund** — $20M+ paid, 2,000+ freed — [raicestexas.org/bond](https://raicestexas.org/bond); Fronterizo Fianza Fund (El Paso); Hutto Community Deportation Defense & Bond Fund (Austin) |
   150→| **Washington** | Fair Fight Immigrant Bond Fund; Northwest Community Bail Fund |
   151→| **Wisconsin** | Community Justice Exchange affiliate |
   152→
   153→> 📋 **Full state-by-state bond fund directory:** [soinformed.substack.com](https://soinformed.substack.com/p/nationwide-bail-funds-and-immigration)
   154→
   155→---
   156→
   157→## 🔍 Part 4: Finding a Detained Loved One
   158→
   159→If someone you know has been arrested by ICE, follow these steps (from [NILC guide](https://www.nilc.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/How-to-Locate-a-ICE-Disappearance.pdf)):
   160→
   161→### Step 1: ICE Online Detainee Locator System
   162→- Search by **A-Number** (alien registration number) + country of birth, OR by name + country of birth
   163→- It can take **24–48 hours** for a name to appear
   164→- **Exceptions**: Children under 18, T/U visa cases, and some transfers may not appear
   165→- 🔗 [locator.ice.gov](https://locator.ice.gov/odls/#/index)
   166→
   167→### Step 2: Contact the ICE Field Office (ERO)
   168→- Call or email your local **ICE Enforcement and Removal Operations** office
   169→- Only provide necessary identifying information (A-number, name, country of birth)
   170→- Ask for the **Deportation Officer** assigned to the case
   171→- 🔗 [ICE Field Office directory](https://www.ice.gov/contact/field-offices)
   172→
   173→### Step 3: Check the Bureau of Prisons (BOP)
   174→- Since 2025, ICE has used BOP facilities to hold immigration detainees
   175→- Search the **BOP Inmate Locator**: [bop.gov/inmateloc](https://www.bop.gov/inmateloc/)
   176→
   177→### Step 4: Contact Consulates
   178→- All immigrants in ICE custody have the right to contact their country's consulate
   179→- ⚠️ **Do NOT contact the consulate** if the person is seeking asylum or fears their government
   180→
   181→### Step 5: Cross-Reference Facility Lists
   182→- **ICE Detention Facilities list**: [ice.gov Detention Statistics page](https://www.ice.gov/detain/detention-management) (Excel spreadsheet at bottom)
   183→- **Freedom for Immigrants**: Searchable list of ICE detention centers with contact info — [freedomforimmigrants.org](https://www.freedomforimmigrants.org/)
   184→
   185→### What to Do When You Find Them
   186→- Get proof of community ties (school records, work verification, volunteer records) for bond hearings
   187→- Ensure they know: **do not sign anything** labeled "waiver," "stipulated removal order," or "voluntary departure agreement" without a lawyer
   188→- If they fear return to home country, they should say so **loudly and clearly** to every officer
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## 📋 Part 5: Family Emergency Preparedness
   193→
   194→### Preparedness Plan Templates
   195→
   196→| Resource | Description | Link |
   197→|---|---|---|
   198→| **ILRC Step-by-Step Family Preparedness Plan** | Comprehensive template covering guardianship, power of attorney, important documents (EN/ES) | [Full version (PDF)](https://www.ilrc.org/sites/default/files/2024-11/Step-by-Step%20Family%20Preparedness%20Plan_ENG_FULL_Nov%202024.pdf) · [Abridged (PDF)](https://www.ilrc.org/sites/default/files/2025-03/Step-by-Step-Family-Preparedness-Plan-Abridged.pdf) |
   199→| **United We Dream Preparedness Packet** | Checklists, contact forms, planning tools for parents and caregivers (EN/ES) | [unitedwedream.org/preparedness-packet](https://unitedwedream.org/resources/stay-ready-with-a-preparedness-packet/) · [Direct PDF](https://unitedwedream.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/20250130-unitedwedream-preparedness-packet-.pdf) |
   200→| **CLINIC Emergency Preparedness (50-State Resource)** | State-by-state guardianship forms, consular info, legal planning | [cliniclegal.org/epif](https://www.cliniclegal.org/epif) |
   201→| **ACLU of Massachusetts Family Preparedness Plan** | Rights-focused family plan | [aclum.org](https://www.aclum.org/know-your-rights/know-your-rights-family-preparedness-plan/) |
   202→| **Informed Immigrant Family Prep Guide** | Steps to take to prepare your family | [informedimmigrant.com](https://www.informedimmigrant.com/resources/know-your-rights/steps-take-prepare-family/) |
   203→| **Montgomery County, MD Resource Page** | Aggregated guides, apps, forms, guardianship project info | [montgomerycountymd.gov](https://www.montgomerycountymd.gov/gilchrist/immigrant-rights/family-preparedness.html) |
   204→| **Ready to Stay** | Family preparedness plan resource | [readytostay.org](https://readytostay.org/immigrants/resources/step-by-step-family-preparedness-plan) |
   205→
   206→### Key Steps for Your Family Plan
   207→
   208→1. **Designate a trusted guardian** for your children in case of detention/deportation
   209→2. **Gather and copy** important documents: birth certificates, passports, immigration papers, medical records, school records
   210→3. **Create a folder labeled "Privileged & Confidential"** — store with a trusted person
   211→4. **Memorize key phone numbers** — attorney, emergency contact, consulate
   212→5. **Download the Notifica app** — sends automatic alerts to emergency contacts if you are detained
   213→6. **Carry your work permit or proof of legal status** (if applicable) — but **never carry a foreign passport** or foreign ID (it can speed up deportation)
   214→7. **Do NOT sign any documents** without talking to a lawyer
   215→8. **Sync photos/videos to the cloud** — in case you record an ICE encounter, others can access the footage
   216→
   217→---
   218→
   219→## 🤝 Part 6: Mutual Aid Networks & Community Support
   220→
   221→### How to Find or Start a Mutual Aid Network
   222→
   223→| Resource | Description | Link |
   224→|---|---|---|
   225→| **Mutual Aid Hub** | Searchable map/directory of mutual aid networks across the US | [mutualaidhub.org](https://www.mutualaidhub.org/) |
   226→| **AFSC: How to Create a Mutual Aid Network** | 5-step guide + history and examples of mutual aid | [afsc.org](https://afsc.org/news/how-create-mutual-aid-network) |
   227→| **Mutual Aid 101 Toolkit** (Mariame Kaba + AOC) | Step-by-step instructions for creating your own network | Included in [AFSC guide](https://afsc.org/news/how-create-mutual-aid-network) |
   228→| **Dean Spade's "Mutual Aid" Book** | Essential reading on building solidarity during crises | Available at most libraries and bookstores |
   229→| **HIAS Action Guide for Congregations** | Guide for faith communities supporting immigrants | [hias.org/take-action](https://hias.org/take-action/) |
   230→
   231→### Types of Mutual Aid You Can Organize
   232→
   233→- **Grocery shopping & supply delivery** for families afraid to leave their homes
   234→- **School drop-off/pick-up watches** — monitor for ICE activity near schools
   235→- **Neighborhood "pods"** — self-organized groups of neighbors for ongoing support
   236→- **Financial solidarity funds** — direct cash assistance to immigrant families
   237→- **Accompaniment programs** — accompanying immigrants to ICE check-ins, hearings, and appointments ([HIAS model](https://hias.org/how/support-our-immigrant-neighbors/))
   238→- **Rapid response volunteering** — joining local rapid response networks to be dispatched when ICE activity is reported
   239→- **Cop-watch / ICE-watch** — community monitoring of enforcement activity in neighborhoods
   240→
   241→### National Mutual Aid & Advocacy Organizations
   242→
   243→| Organization | Focus | Link |
   244→|---|---|---|
   245→| **United We Dream** | Largest immigrant youth-led network; preparedness resources, KYR, advocacy | [unitedwedream.org](https://unitedwedream.org/) |
   246→| **HIAS** | Refugee resettlement, pro bono legal, accompaniment, Welcome Circles | [hias.org/support-our-immigrant-neighbors](https://hias.org/how/support-our-immigrant-neighbors/) |
   247→| **American Friends Service Committee (AFSC)** | Mutual aid organizing, immigrant rights, community defense | [afsc.org](https://afsc.org/) |
   248→| **Freedom for Immigrants** | Detention visitation, bond assistance, detainee hotline | [freedomforimmigrants.org](https://www.freedomforimmigrants.org/) |
   249→| **No More Deaths / No Más Muertes** | Direct aid to migrants in the US-Mexico borderlands | [nomoredeaths.org](https://nomoredeaths.org/) |
   250→| **Win Without War** | Curated mutual aid & immigration resource hub | [winwithoutwar.org/immigration-mutual-aid](https://winwithoutwar.org/policy/immigration-mutual-aid/) |
   251→| **National Network for Immigrant & Refugee Rights (NNIRR)** | Hotline aggregator, rapid response coordination | [nnirr.org](https://nnirr.org/) |
   252→| **California Collaborative for Immigrant Justice (CCIJ)** | Statewide rapid response network finder for CA | [ccijustice.org/carrn](https://www.ccijustice.org/carrn) |
   253→| **League of Women Voters of California** | Rapid response directory, KYR, Red Cards | [lwvc.org](https://lwvc.org/ice-activity-escalating-resources-protect-communities/) |
   254→| **National COSH (Safety Without Borders)** | Workplace safety rights for immigrant workers regardless of status | [nationalcosh.org/Safety-Without-Borders](https://nationalcosh.org/Safety-Without-Borders) |
   255→
   256→---
   257→
   258→## 👷 Part 7: Worker Rights & Workplace Protections
   259→
   260→Regardless of immigration status, all workers in the U.S. have rights:
   261→
   262→| Resource | Description | Link |
   263→|---|---|---|
   264→| **DOJ Immigrant & Employee Rights Section (IER)** | Handles discrimination complaints based on citizenship/immigration status; **Hotline: 1-800-255-7688** (Mon–Fri, 9 AM – 5 PM ET) | [justice.gov/crt/ier](https://www.justice.gov/crt/immigrant-and-employee-rights-section) |
   265→| **National COSH: Safety Without Borders** | Workers' rights to a safe workplace; OSHA protections apply regardless of status | [nationalcosh.org](https://nationalcosh.org/Safety-Without-Borders) |
   266→| **NYC DCWP Know Your Worker Rights** | Worker rights guide for NYC (wage theft, safe/sick leave, freelancer protections) | [nyc.gov/know-your-worker-rights](https://www.nyc.gov/site/dca/workers/workersrights/know-your-worker-rights.page) |
   267→| **Temporary Worker Rights (State Dept.)** | Rights and protections for temporary workers | [travel.state.gov](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/us-visas/visa-information-resources/wilberforce-english.html) |
   268→
   269→---
   270→
   271→## 📚 Part 8: Comprehensive Resource Hubs
   272→
   273→These are "meta-resources" that aggregate many of the above into one place:
   274→
   275→| Resource | Description | Link |
   276→|---|---|---|
   277→| **Rep. Tlaib's 2026 National Resource List** | Extensive national list: legal aid, mutual aid, bond funds, rapid response, worker rights | [rashidaforcongress.com](https://rashidaforcongress.com/2025-resources-to-defend-immigrants-and-other-neighbors-in-your-community/) |
   278→| **Win Without War: Immigration & Mutual Aid Resources** | Curated list of legal, mutual aid, know your rights, and policy resources | [winwithoutwar.org](https://winwithoutwar.org/policy/immigration-mutual-aid/) |
   279→| **Austin Kocher KYR Resource Library** | Organized by scenario (home, work, public); includes video resources | [austinkocher.substack.com](https://austinkocher.substack.com/p/know-your-rights-resources-for-ice) |
   280→| **Informed Immigrant** | Guides, toolkits, and news for immigrants | [informedimmigrant.com](https://www.informedimmigrant.com/) |
   281→| **Nationwide Bail Funds & Immigration Support by State** | State-by-state directory of bail funds and immigration support | [soinformed.substack.com](https://soinformed.substack.com/p/nationwide-bail-funds-and-immigration) |
   282→| **HIAS: Seven Ways to Take Action for Immigrants** | Actionable guide for allies (KYR, legal referrals, rapid response, accompaniment) | [hias.org](https://hias.org/news/seven-ways-take-action-immigrants/) |
   283→| **LWVC: ICE Activity Escalating — Resources** | California-focused but nationally applicable; rapid response, Red Cards, KYR | [lwvc.org](https://lwvc.org/ice-activity-escalating-resources-protect-communities/) |
   284→
   285→---
   286→
   287→## 🛡️ Quick-Reference: Know Your Rights Cards
   288→
   289→| Resource | Description | Link |
   290→|---|---|---|
   291→| **ILRC Red Cards (Tarjetas Rojas)** | Pocket-sized rights cards in **56 languages** — 10M+ distributed. Print your own or order. | [ilrc.org/know-your-rights](https://www.ilrc.org/community-resources/know-your-rights) |
   292→| **United We Dream KYR Card** | Printable "Know Your Power" card for wallet | [unitedwedream.org/know-your-rights](https://unitedwedream.org/resources/know-your-rights/) |
   293→| **ACLU KYR Guide** | Multi-language rights guide | [aclu.org/kyr-request](https://action.aclu.org/webform/kyr-request) |
   294→
   295→---
   296→
   297→## 🔑 Key Reminders
   298→
   299→- **You have constitutional rights regardless of immigration status** — including the right to remain silent, the right to an attorney, and the right to refuse entry without a judicial warrant
   300→- **Do not open the door** for ICE unless they show a **judicial warrant signed by a judge** (not an administrative ICE warrant)
   301→- **Do not sign anything** without speaking to a lawyer
   302→- **Do not carry a foreign passport** — it can accelerate deportation
   303→- **Preparation is power** — complete a family preparedness plan and store it with a trusted person today
   304→- **Save your local rapid response hotline** in your phone now, before an emergency
   305→
   306→---
   307→
   308→*This page was compiled on February 9, 2026 from live web sources. Check individual organization websites for the most current information and resources.*
   309→

content.content:
1→# Know Your Rights & Legal Observer Training — ICE (February 2026)
     2→
     3→> **Last Updated:** February 9, 2026
     4→> A comprehensive resource guide for Know Your Rights (KYR) trainings, Legal/Constitutional Observer trainings, and related resources in response to escalating U.S. immigration enforcement.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## 📌 Current Context: Why This Is Surging Now
     9→
    10→Immigration enforcement has escalated dramatically across the United States since early 2025, and especially in early 2026 following high-profile incidents in Minneapolis. In response, a nationwide movement of Know Your Rights trainings and Legal/Constitutional Observer trainings has surged:
    11→
    12→- Nearly **30,000 Minnesotans** have been trained as constitutional observers, with the Immigrant Defense Network averaging over **2,000 people trained per week**
    13→- The No Kings coalition's first "Eyes on ICE" virtual training on January 26, 2026 drew over **200,000 viewers**, with a follow-up session held on February 5
    14→- In Wisconsin, one organizer opened 45 spots that filled in a day, expanded to 100 — which also sold out with 50 on the waitlist
    15→- A FOX News poll found more than half of voters believe ICE tactics are "too aggressive"
    16→- A Pew Research Center poll found about 74% of Americans say it's acceptable to record immigration officers
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## 🛡️ Part 1: Know Your Rights (KYR) Training
    21→
    22→### What KYR Trainings Cover
    23→
    24→KYR trainings educate individuals — particularly immigrants and community members — on their constitutional rights regardless of immigration status.
    25→
    26→#### At Home
    27→- You are **not required to open the door** for ICE
    28→- Agents need a **judicial warrant signed by a judge** (not an administrative ICE warrant) to enter
    29→- Ask to see the warrant through a window or slipped under the door
    30→
    31→#### In Public
    32→- You have the **right to remain silent**
    33→- Ask: *"Am I being detained?"* and *"Am I free to go?"*
    34→- Do not run
    35→- Do not sign anything without a lawyer
    36→
    37→#### At Work
    38→- ICE may enter public-facing areas (lobbies, parking lots) without a warrant
    39→- ICE **cannot access private/employee-only areas** without a judicial search warrant or employer consent
    40→
    41→#### If Detained
    42→- You retain the right to **remain silent**
    43→- You have the right to a **phone call**
    44→- You have the right to an **attorney**
    45→- **Do not sign any documents** without legal counsel
    46→
    47→#### Data & Privacy
    48→- Guidance on protecting personal data and phone security during encounters
    49→
    50→---
    51→
    52→### Key KYR Resources (Updated for 2026)
    53→
    54→| Organization | Resource | Languages | Link |
    55→|---|---|---|---|
    56→| **ACLU** | Immigrants' Rights: Know Your Rights guide; "We Have Rights" video series | 8+ languages | [aclu.org/webform/kyr-request](https://action.aclu.org/webform/kyr-request) |
    57→| **ILRC** | Red Cards (Tarjetas Rojas) — 10M+ distributed | **56 languages** | [ilrc.org/know-your-rights](https://www.ilrc.org/community-resources/know-your-rights) |
    58→| **Immigrant Defense Project (IDP)** | "Don't Get ICEd" interactive tool; KYR workshops | English, Spanish | [immigrantdefenseproject.org/know-your-rights](https://www.immigrantdefenseproject.org/know-your-rights../) |
    59→| **National Immigrant Justice Center (NIJC)** | "If You Encounter ICE" comprehensive guide | Multiple | [immigrantjustice.org/ice-encounter](https://immigrantjustice.org/for-immigrants/know-your-rights/ice-encounter/) |
    60→| **Informed Immigrant** | Scenario-based guides (home, work, vehicle, airport) | Multiple | [informedimmigrant.com/guides/know-your-rights](https://www.informedimmigrant.com/guides/know-your-rights/) |
    61→| **NYIC** | Updated KYR curriculum + roleplaying exercises | English, Spanish | [nyic.org/resources-training/kyr](https://www.nyic.org/resources-training/kyr/) |
    62→| **Austin Kocher (Substack)** | Comprehensive 2026-updated resource library organized by scenario | N/A (meta-resource) | [austinkocher.substack.com](https://austinkocher.substack.com/p/know-your-rights-resources-for-ice) |
    63→| **ACLU of SoCal** | "ICE Agents at Your Door" guide | English, Spanish | [aclusocal.org/ice-agents-your-door](https://www.aclusocal.org/know-your-rights/ice-agents-your-door/) |
    64→| **ACLU NorCal** | "Know Your Rights: If ICE Confronts You" | Multiple | [aclunorcal.org](https://www.aclunorcal.org/know-your-rights/know-your-rights-if-ice-confronts-you/) |
    65→| **NWIRP** | Northwest Immigrant Rights Project KYR | Multiple | [nwirp.org/resources/kyr](https://nwirp.org/resources/kyr/) |
    66→| **PA Immigration Coalition** | ICE Resistance Resources | English, Spanish | [paimmigrant.org/ice-resistance-resources](https://www.paimmigrant.org/ice-resistance-resources) |
    67→
    68→---
    69→
    70→### Upcoming & Active KYR Training Sessions (as of Feb 9, 2026)
    71→
    72→| Date | Event | Organizer | Details |
    73→|---|---|---|---|
    74→| **Feb 10, 2026** | "Know Your Responsibilities" Training | ACLU of Alaska | Zoom — [acluak.org/events](https://www.acluak.org/events/) |
    75→| **Feb 17, 2026** | Immigration Bystander Training | ACLU of Alaska | Zoom — [acluak.org/events](https://www.acluak.org/events/) |
    76→| **Feb 17, 2026 (4 PM CT)** | Know Your Rights: Immigration Policy Updates | City of Austin + American Gateways + TxILC | Zoom, Spanish interpretation — [austintexas.gov](https://www.austintexas.gov/event/know-your-rights-immigration-policy-updates) |
    77→| **Feb 19, 2026 (3–4 PM)** | Know Your Rights: Constitutional Protections and Immigration Enforcement | AFL-CIO | Virtual — [region9a.uaw.org](https://region9a.uaw.org/events/AFLCIOFrontlineTraining021926) |
    78→| **March 10, 2026 (4 PM CT)** | Know Your Rights: Immigration Policy Updates | City of Austin + American Gateways + TxILC | Zoom, Spanish interpretation — [austintexas.gov](https://www.austintexas.gov/event/know-your-rights-immigration-policy-updates-1) |
    79→| **Ongoing / On Request** | ACLU National KYR Trainings | ACLU | For groups of 15+; English & Spanish; 2-week lead time — [aclu.org/kyr-request](https://action.aclu.org/webform/kyr-request) |
    80→| **Recorded** | ACLU of Maine KYR Webinar ft. U.S. Magistrate Judge James Orenstein & fmr. U.S. Attorney Joyce Vance | ACLU of Maine | [aclumaine.org](https://www.aclumaine.org/know-your-rights-training-ice-privacy-protests/) |
    81→| **Recorded** | Tribal Communities KYR Webinar | NARF / NCAI | [localnewsmatters.org](https://localnewsmatters.org/2026/02/06/tribal-communities-know-your-rights-ice-webinar/) |
    82→
    83→---
    84→
    85→## 👁️ Part 2: Legal Observer / Constitutional Observer Training
    86→
    87→### What It Is
    88→
    89→A **Constitutional Observer** (also called a legal observer or ICE observer) is a trained community member who **documents law enforcement and immigration enforcement activity** without interfering. The practice is rooted in the National Lawyers Guild's Legal Observer® Program, established in 1968, and draws from the Black Panther Party's 1966 copwatch patrols in Oakland.
    90→
    91→Today's observers are specifically focused on ICE and CBP operations.
    92→
    93→---
    94→
    95→### What Constitutional Observer Trainings Teach
    96→
    97→Based on the **COPAL MN / Immigrant Defense Network Handbook** — the standard training material used across Minnesota and increasingly nationwide.
    98→
    99→#### Core Rights of Observers
   100→- Recording law enforcement activity in public is **lawful and protected by the First Amendment**
   101→- You have the right to remain silent and to observe from a safe distance
   102→
   103→#### Safety Protocols
   104→- Maintain at least **8 feet** from ICE agents at all times
   105→- **Never obstruct or interfere** with enforcement operations
   106→- If asked to step back, comply immediately and document that you are doing so
   107→- Wear visible identification (yellow vests, observer badges) when possible
   108→
   109→#### The S.A.L.U.T.E. Documentation Framework
   110→
   111→| Letter | Meaning | What to Record |
   112→|---|---|---|
   113→| **S** | Size | Number of agents and vehicles |
   114→| **A** | Activity | Specific actions, especially suspicious ones |
   115→| **L** | Location | Address, landmarks, cross streets |
   116→| **U** | Uniform | Clothing, armor, insignia, markings |
   117→| **T** | Time | Continuous timestamps for extended events |
   118→| **E** | Equipment | Weapons, gear, tactical equipment |
   119→
   120→#### After Recording
   121→- Immediately copy video files to a second device **without renaming** the original
   122→- Edit only the copy
   123→- Notify the Immigrant Defense Network or your local organization so they can track and assist the person detained
   124→- **Do not post** video that clearly identifies a detained individual on social media — share it with the person's family, friends, or lawyer first
   125→
   126→#### De-escalation
   127→- Control yourself first, then help calm those around you
   128→- The goal is **recording, not confrontation**
   129→
   130→---
   131→
   132→### Key Legal Observer Training Programs & Sign-Ups
   133→
   134→| Program | Organizer | Details & Sign-Up |
   135→|---|---|---|
   136→| **"Eyes on ICE: Document and Record"** | No Kings Coalition / ACLU | National virtual trainings; ongoing sessions — [nokings.org/trainings](https://www.nokings.org/trainings) |
   137→| **Eyes on ICE (YouTube Recordings)** | No Kings Coalition | Jan 26 session — [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LHDgNd4ibVc) · Feb 5 session — [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4xBUa67XAyM) |
   138→| **Constitutional Observer Training** | Minnesota DFL | Statewide sessions; next: **Feb 24, 2026 at 7 PM** — [dfl.org/observers](https://dfl.org/observers/) |
   139→| **Constitutional Observer Training** | Immigrant Defense Network (IDN) | Originator of the constitutional observer model in MN — [Sign-Up Form](https://secure.ngpvan.com/eo_JwdjrLkSJi3cvrBBjFg2) · [immigrantdefensenetwork.org](https://immigrantdefensenetwork.org) |
   140→| **Legal Observation Project** | NY Attorney General Letitia James | State-run program sending trained OAG observers in purple vests; accepts public video submissions — [ag.ny.gov announcement](https://ag.ny.gov/press-release/2026/attorney-general-james-launches-legal-observation-project-monitor-federal) |
   141→| **NLG Legal Observer Program** | National Lawyers Guild | Established 1968; chapters nationwide — [nlg.org/los](https://www.nlg.org/massdefenseprogram/los/) |
   142→| **NLG San Francisco Bay Area** | NLG-SFBA | Immigration Court Observation + KYR train-the-trainer — [nlgsf.org](https://nlgsf.org/programs-committees/nlg-sfba-programs/immigration-justice-program/) |
   143→| **Legal Observer Training** | Rutgers Center for Immigration Justice | Hosted training sessions — [cij.rutgers.edu](https://cij.rutgers.edu/legal-observer-training/) |
   144→| **ACLU People Power** | ACLU | Grassroots network with monthly volunteer action calls — [aclu.org/people-power](https://www.aclu.org/campaigns-initiatives/people-power) |
   145→| **ACLU NorCal Training Program** | ACLU NorCal | Empowering immigrant communities training — [aclunorcal.org](https://www.aclunorcal.org/news/inside-aclu-norcal-s-training-program-empower-immigrant-communities-take-action/) |
   146→| **Civil Liberties Defense Center** | CLDC (Oregon) | Legal Observer Training + "Know Your Rights & Risks" — [cldc.org/events](https://cldc.org/events/) |
   147→| **"How to Legally Observe ICE"** | Women's March / Action Network | Training session — [actionnetwork.org](https://actionnetwork.org/events/stop-ice-how-to-be-a-legal-observer-training?nowrapper=true) |
   148→| **ACLU KYR Training (Protest Safety)** | ACLU National | Protest safety, KYR, and de-escalation — [act.aclu.org](https://act.aclu.org/a/aclukyr) |
   149→| **All Eyes on ICE** | University of North Dakota | Campus training session — [calendar.und.edu](https://calendar.und.edu/event/all-eyes-on-ice) |
   150→| **Northern Lights Indivisible** | Indivisible (WI) | Legal Observer Training in the Northwoods, WI — [wxpr.org](https://www.wxpr.org/politics-government/2026-02-03/northern-lights-indivisible-holds-legal-observer-training-in-the-northwoods) |
   151→
   152→---
   153→
   154→### The COPAL MN Constitutional Observer Handbook
   155→
   156→This has become the **de facto national standard** for observer trainings. It was developed by COPAL MN and the Immigrant Defense Network and is referenced by training sessions run by the Minnesota DFL, Indivisible chapters, and groups in Iowa, Wisconsin, North Dakota, and beyond.
   157→
   158→📥 **Download:** [copalmn.org/the-handbook-for-constitutional-observers](https://copalmn.org/the-handbook-for-constitutional-observers/)
   159→
   160→📥 **Direct PDF:** [copalmn.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/PDF-Handbook.pdf](https://copalmn.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/PDF-Handbook.pdf)
   161→
   162→---
   163→
   164→## ⚖️ Part 3: Important Legal & Safety Considerations
   165→
   166→### ✅ What Observers CAN Legally Do
   167→- Record in public spaces (sidewalks, streets) — this is a **First Amendment right**
   168→- Verbally inform individuals of their rights (e.g., "You have the right to remain silent")
   169→- Use whistles, car horns, or flashers to alert community members
   170→
   171→### ❌ What Observers Should NOT Do
   172→- Physically obstruct or interfere with ICE operations — this can result in **arrest for obstruction**
   173→- Post unverified information on social media
   174→- Put themselves in the path of vehicles or agents
   175→
   176→### ⚠️ Risks to Be Aware Of
   177→- While observing is lawful, the current environment carries real safety risks
   178→- An internal ICE memo has revealed agents are being trained to **force entry into homes without judicial warrants**, complicating traditional KYR advice about not opening the door
   179→- Experts advise: *"You have to be realistic about what level of risk you're willing to accept"*
   180→
   181→---
   182→
   183→## 📚 Part 4: Additional Resources
   184→
   185→### Video & Multimedia
   186→| Resource | Link |
   187→|---|---|
   188→| ACLU "We Have Rights" Video Series (8 languages — narrated by Jesse Williams, Diane Guerrero, Kumail Nanjiani, and others) | [austinkocher.substack.com (resource list)](https://austinkocher.substack.com/p/know-your-rights-resources-for-ice) |
   189→| WITNESS + NYIC "Practical Tips on Filming Immigration and Law Enforcement" | [austinkocher.substack.com (resource list)](https://austinkocher.substack.com/p/know-your-rights-resources-for-ice) |
   190→| YouTube: "What is a constitutional observer, and how are they trained?" | [youtube.com](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0eBrVDIIZA8) |
   191→| YouTube: Hundreds attend ICE legal observer training | [youtube.com](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AEmcKD-VQr8) |
   192→| YouTube: No Kings "Eyes on ICE" Session 1 (Jan 26) | [youtube.com](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LHDgNd4ibVc) |
   193→| YouTube: No Kings "Eyes on ICE" Session 2 (Feb 5) | [youtube.com](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4xBUa67XAyM) |
   194→
   195→### Organizations & Legal Help
   196→| Resource | Link |
   197→|---|---|
   198→| No Kings Coalition — Trainings Hub | [nokings.org/trainings](https://www.nokings.org/trainings) |
   199→| Immigrant Defense Network | [immigrantdefensenetwork.org](https://immigrantdefensenetwork.org) |
   200→| COPAL MN — Full Resource Library | [copalmn.org/resources](https://copalmn.org/resources/) |
   201→| ILRC — Non-profit Legal Services Directory (searchable by state, county, or detention facility) | [ilrc.org/know-your-rights](https://www.ilrc.org/community-resources/know-your-rights) |
   202→| PA Immigration Coalition — ICE Resistance Resources | [paimmigrant.org](https://www.paimmigrant.org/ice-resistance-resources) |
   203→| Fennemore Law — Preparing for ICE Workplace Enforcement Actions | [fennemorelaw.com](https://www.fennemorelaw.com/being-prepared-for-ice-workplace-enforcement-actions/) |
   204→| ACLU of Nevada — Immigrants' Rights | [aclunv.org](https://www.aclunv.org/know-your-rights/immigrants-rights/) |
   205→
   206→### News & Analysis
   207→| Article | Source | Link |
   208→|---|---|---|
   209→| What we know about ICE watch groups trained to observe federal agents | CNN (Jan 22, 2026) | [cnn.com](https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/22/us/ice-watch-observers-federal-agents) |
   210→| Minnesota sees surge in constitutional observer training demand | KSTP (Jan 27, 2026) | [kstp.com](https://kstp.com/kstp-news/top-news/minnesota-sees-surge-in-constitutional-observer-training-demand-after-federal-agent-shooting) |
   211→| Minnesotans are training to become constitutional observers — of ICE | NHPR (Jan 28, 2026) | [nhpr.org](https://www.nhpr.org/2026-01-29/minnesotans-are-training-to-become-constitutional-observers-of-ice) |
   212→| Nearly 30,000 Minnesotans trained as constitutional observers | MPR News (Feb 2, 2026) | [mprnews.org](https://www.mprnews.org/story/2026/02/02/immigrant-defense-network-training-constitutional-observers) |
   213→| Northern Lights Indivisible holds legal observer training in the Northwoods | WXPR (Feb 3, 2026) | [wxpr.org](https://www.wxpr.org/politics-government/2026-02-03/northern-lights-indivisible-holds-legal-observer-training-in-the-northwoods) |
   214→| "Know your rights" efforts hampered by reality of ICE's forced-entry tactics | News-Press (Jan 30, 2026) | [newspress.com](https://www.newspress.com/2026/01/30/know-your-rights-efforts-hampered-by-reality-of-ices-forced-entry-tactics/) |
   215→| Local groups are offering training on how to observe ICE, CBP | Public Radio Tulsa (Feb 2, 2026) | [publicradiotulsa.org](https://www.publicradiotulsa.org/local-regional/2026-02-02/local-groups-are-offering-training-on-how-to-observe-ice-cbp) |
   216→| No Kings Coalition responds to escalating brutality | ACLU (Jan 27, 2026) | [aclu.org](https://www.aclu.org/press-releases/no-kings-coalition-responds-to-escalating-brutality) |
   217→| AG James launches Legal Observation Project | NY Attorney General (Feb 2, 2026) | [ag.ny.gov](https://ag.ny.gov/press-release/2026/attorney-general-james-launches-legal-observation-project-monitor-federal) |
   218→
   219→---
   220→
   221→## 🔑 Key Takeaway
   222→
   223→The core message across all programs is consistent: **know your constitutional rights, document everything, never interfere, and stay safe.** Whether you are an immigrant seeking to understand your protections or a community member wanting to serve as an observer, multiple organizations — from the ACLU and NLG to local grassroots groups — are actively offering free training, with new sessions being added weekly.
   224→
   225→---
   226→
   227→*This page was compiled on February 9, 2026 from live web sources. Check individual organization websites for the most current schedules and registration links.*

content.content:
1→name: Deploy to AWS
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: [main]
     6→  workflow_dispatch:
     7→
     8→concurrency:
     9→  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
    10→  cancel-in-progress: true
    11→
    12→jobs:
    13→  build-and-deploy:
    14→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    15→    timeout-minutes: 15
    16→
    17→    steps:
    18→      - name: Checkout code
    19→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    20→
    21→      - name: Setup Node.js
    22→        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    23→        with:
    24→          node-version: '20'
    25→          cache: 'npm'
    26→
    27→      - name: Install dependencies
    28→        run: npm ci
    29→
    30→      - name: Build
    31→        run: npm run build
    32→
    33→      - name: Deploy static files
    34→        uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7
    35→        with:
    36→          host: ${{ secrets.AWS_HOST }}
    37→          username: ${{ secrets.AWS_USER }}
    38→          key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SSH_KEY }}
    39→          source: "dist/*"
    40→          target: "/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/gifteddreamers.org"
    41→          strip_components: 1
    42→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1→name: CI
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: [main]
     6→  pull_request:
     7→    branches: [main]
     8→
     9→concurrency:
    10→  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
    11→  cancel-in-progress: true
    12→
    13→jobs:
    14→  build:
    15→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    16→    timeout-minutes: 10
    17→
    18→    steps:
    19→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    20→
    21→      - name: Setup Node.js
    22→        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    23→        with:
    24→          node-version: '20'
    25→          cache: 'npm'
    26→
    27→      - name: Install dependencies
    28→        run: npm ci
    29→
    30→      - name: Type check
    31→        run: npx tsc --noEmit
    32→        continue-on-error: true
    33→
    34→      - name: Build
    35→        run: npm run build
    36→

What is a matching gift?

10→ 11→// Double the Donation plugin configuration 12→const DTD_API_KEY = '6HMm5sEaYqgnLZmU'; 13→ 14→// n8n webhook URL for volunteer form submissions 15→const WEBHOOK_URL = 'https://n8n.cloudpublica.org/webhook/volunteer-form'; 16→ 17→interface FormData { 18→ firstName: string; 19→ lastName: string; 20→ email: string; 21→ company: string; 22→ skills: string[]; 23→ message: string; 24→} 25→ 26→const skillOptions = [ 27→ { value: 'sysadmin', label: 'System Administration' }, 28→ { value: 'security', label: 'Security / DevSecOps' }, 29→ { value: 'ai-ml', label: 'AI / Machine Learning' }, 30→ { value: 'automation', label: 'Automation / n8n' }, 31→ { value: 'accounting', label: 'Accounting / Bookkeeping' }, 32→ { value: 'content', label: 'Content Creation' }, 33→ { value: 'social-media', label: 'Social Media' }, 34→ { value: 'design', label: 'Graphic Design' }, 35→ { value: 'video', label: 'Video Production' }, 36→ { value: 'community', label: 'Community Management' }, 37→]; 38→ 39→const Volunteer: React.FC = () => { 40→ // Load Double the Donation plugin 41→ useEffect(() => { 42→ // Load the DTD script 43→ const script = document.createElement('script'); 44→ script.src = 'https://doublethedonation.com/api/js/ddplugin.js'; 45→ script.async = true; 46→ script.onload = () => { 47→ // Initialize the plugin after script loads 48→ const pluginNode = document.getElementById('dtd-plugin'); 49→ if (pluginNode && (window as any).doublethedonation?.plugin?.v2?.load_plugin) { 50→ const config = { sections: ['volunteer', 'match', 'payroll-giving'] }; 51→ (window as any).doublethedonation.plugin.v2.load_plugin(pluginNode, DTD_API_KEY, config); 52→ } 53→ }; 54→ document.head.appendChild(script); 55→ 56→ return () => { 57→ // Cleanup script on unmount 58→ const existingScript = document.querySelector('script[src="https://doublethedonation.com/api/js/ddplugin.js"]'); 59→ if (existingScript) { 60→ existingScript.remove(); 61→ } 62→ }; 63→ }, []); 64→ 65→ const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ 66→ firstName: '', 67→ lastName: '', 68→ email: '', 69→ company: '', 70→ skills: [], 71→ message: '', 72→ }); 73→ const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false); 74→ const [submitStatus, setSubmitStatus] = useState<'idle' | 'success' | 'error'>('idle'); 75→ const [errorMessage, setErrorMessage] = useState(''); 76→ 77→ const handleInputChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent) => { 78→ const { name, value } = e.target; 79→ setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, [name]: value })); 80→ }; 81→ 82→ const handleSkillToggle = (skill: string) => { 83→ setFormData(prev => ({ 84→ ...prev, 85→ skills: prev.skills.includes(skill) 86→ ? prev.skills.filter(s => s !== skill) 87→ : [...prev.skills, skill] 88→ })); 89→ }; 90→ 91→ const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => { 92→ e.preventDefault(); 93→ setIsSubmitting(true); 94→ setSubmitStatus('idle'); 95→ setErrorMessage(''); 96→ 97→ try { 98→ const response = await fetch(WEBHOOK_URL, { 99→ method: 'POST', 100→ headers: { 101→ 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 102→ }, 103→ body: JSON.stringify({ 104→ firstName: formData.firstName, 105→ lastName: formData.lastName, 106→ email: formData.email, 107→ company: formData.company, 108→ skills: formData.skills, 109→ message: formData.message, 110→ submittedAt: new Date().toISOString(), 111→ source: 'v3gifteddreamers', 112→ }), 113→ }); 114→ 115→ if (response.ok) { 116→ setSubmitStatus('success'); 117→ setFormData({ 118→ firstName: '', 119→ lastName: '', 120→ email: '', 121→ company: '', 122→ skills: [], 123→ message: '', 124→ }); 125→ } else { 126→ throw new Error('Failed to submit form'); 127→ } 128→ } catch (error) { 129→ setSubmitStatus('error'); 130→ setErrorMessage('There was an error submitting your application. Please try again or email volunteer@gifteddreamers.org'); 131→ } finally { 132→ setIsSubmitting(false); 133→ } 134→ }; 135→ 136→ return ( 137→

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile a detailed report of my findings.

## Exploration Summary: Gifted Dreamers v3 Site Structure and Design

I've thoroughly explored the Gifted Dreamers website codebase. Here's a comprehensive analysis:

---

## 1. HTML STRUCTURE & KEY FILES

**Main HTML File:** `/index.html`
- **DOCTYPE:** HTML5
- **Language:** English (en)
- **Responsive:** Includes viewport meta tag for mobile-first design
- **Performance Optimized:** Inlined critical CSS, preloading strategies, and lazy loading for third-party scripts

**Key Meta Tags:**
- SEO-optimized with keywords targeting nonprofit technology, tech perks, Google Ad Grants
- Open Graph tags for social sharing
- Twitter Card support
- JSON-LD structured data for organizational schema
- Canonical URL: https://gifteddreamers.org

**Site Organization:**
- **Tax ID (EIN):** 39-3863796
- **Status:** 501(c)(3) nonprofit
- **Founder:** Kristine Socall (Executive Director)
- **Email:** services@gifteddreamers.org
- **Region:** Texas, USA

---

## 2. CSS STYLING & BRANDING

### Color Palette
**Primary Blue (Brand Blue):**
- `#0D729C` - Primary/Default
- `#108BC7` - Light variant (hover states)
- `#0A5A7D` - Dark variant

**Brand Red:**
- `#A22131` - Accent red (appears in logo text)

**Brand Light Blue:**
- `#00B4D8` - Light brand blue (appears in logo text)

**Supporting Colors:**
- Slate colors: `#0f172a` (footer background), `#e2e8f0` (borders)
- White/Light: `#ffffff` with transparency
- Dark text: `#0f172a`

### Typography
- **Font Family:** Inter (custom weights: 300, 400, 500, 600, 700)
- **Fallbacks:** -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, sans-serif
- **Line Behavior:** Smooth scrolling enabled
- **Font Loading:** Async-loaded from Google Fonts to prevent render blocking

### Tailwind Configuration
**File:** `tailwind.config.js`
```javascript
- Uses Tailwind CSS v4.1.18
- Custom theme extensions for brand colors
- Content scanning: HTML, JS, TS, JSX, TSX files
```

---

## 3. PAGE LAYOUT & STRUCTURE

### Main Layout (App.tsx)
- **Router:** React Router v7 with lazy loading for all pages
- **Structure:** Navbar → Breadcrumbs → Main Content → Footer
- **Accessibility:** Skip link for keyboard navigation
- **Analytics:** Privacy-first approach (consent-based loading)

### Navigation Structure
**Pages:**
1. `/` - Home
2. `/services` - Tech Perks Audit, Accounting Cleanup
3. `/volunteer` - Volunteer Opportunities
4. `/gruhp` - GRUHP Fund
5. `/corporate-partners` - Corporate Partners
6. `/common-cloud` - Common Cloud Resources
7. `/about` - About Page
8. `/matching-gifts` - Matching Gifts
9. `/faq` - FAQ
10. `/contact` - Contact Form
11. `/privacy` - Privacy Policy
12. `/terms` - Terms of Service

---

## 4. FOOTER SECTION (CRITICAL DETAILS)

**Footer Component:** `/components/Footer.tsx`

### Footer Structure
- **Background Color:** `#0f172a` (slate-900)
- **Text Color:** `#cbd5e1` (slate-300)
- **Fixed Height:** 1100px (prevents layout shift during loading)
- **Containment:** Uses CSS containment for performance optimization

### Footer Content Organization (4-Column Grid)

**Column 1: SERVICES**
- Tech Perks Audit
- Accounting Cleanup
- Book a Session

**Column 2: VOLUNTEER**
- Volunteer Opportunities
- Corporate Partners

**Column 3: GIVE BACK**
- GRUHP Fund
- Matching Gifts

**Column 4: LEARN**
- Common Cloud
- Substack
- Resources

### Organization Info Section
- **Logo:** Displayed at footer (32px × 32px)
- **Branding:** "Gifted Dreamers" with inline styles
  - "Gifted" in brand red (#E8505B)
  - "Dreamers" in sky blue (#38BDF8)
- **Legal Text:** 
```
Gifted Dreamers, Inc. is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.
EIN: 39-3863796 | All donations are tax-deductible.
© 2025 Gifted Dreamers, Inc. All rights reserved.
```

### Footer Links
- About
- Contact
- FAQ

### Social Media Icons
**9 Social Channels** (44×44px touch targets for accessibility):
1. Substack (substack.png)
2. LinkedIn (linkedin.svg)
3. Facebook (facebook.svg)
4. Instagram (instagram.svg)
5. YouTube (youtube.svg)
6. TikTok (tiktok.svg)
7. X/Twitter (X.svg)
8. Pinterest (pinterest.svg)

**Icon Styling:**
- Hover effects: 80% opacity + scale-110 transform
- SVG/PNG format for optimal rendering
- Lazy loading for performance
- Min-height: 99px to reserve space

---

## 5. NAVBAR COMPONENT

**File:** `/components/Navbar.tsx`

### Navigation Structure
- **Position:** Sticky (top: 0, z-index: 100)
- **Background:** White/95% opacity with 12px blur backdrop filter
- **Height:** h-20 (80px)
- **Border:** 1px bottom border in slate-200

### Navigation Links (6 Main Items)
1. Services
2. Volunteer
3. GRUHP
4. Matching Gifts
5. Partners
6. Common Cloud

### Button
- "Contact Us" button with primary styling
- Arrow icon on mobile/dropdown
- Responsive design: full-width on mobile, inline on desktop

### Responsive Behavior
- **Desktop:** Horizontal menu + dropdown (56px width)
- **Mobile:** Full-screen menu with hamburger toggle
- **Active State:** Primary blue background with rounded corners

---

## 6. DESIGN PATTERNS & PATTERNS

### Button Component (`Button.tsx`)
**Variants:**
- `primary` - Blue background with light blue hover
- `accent` - Accent blue styling
- `outline` - Border style with no fill
- `ghost` - Transparent with hover background

**Sizes:**
- `sm` - 36px height, 12px padding
- `md` - 44px height, 24px padding (default)
- `lg` - 56px height, 32px padding

**States:** Focus rings, disabled state, active scale animation

### Reveal Component (`Reveal.tsx`)
- **Purpose:** Scroll-triggered animations
- **Mechanism:** IntersectionObserver API
- **Animation:** Fade-in + slide-up on scroll
- **Delay Support:** Staggered animations
- **CSS Classes:**
  - `reveal-hidden` - Initial state (opacity: 0, translateY: 20px)
  - `reveal-visible` - Animated state (opacity: 1, translateY: 0)
  - **Transition:** 0.7s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1)

### Performance Optimizations
- **Critical CSS:** Inlined in `<head>` for above-the-fold content
- **Footer Containment:** `contain: layout style paint` prevents reflow
- **Hero Image:** Aspect ratio 16:9 to prevent CLS
- **Lazy Loading:** Third-party scripts load after user consent
- **DNS Prefetch:** Google Analytics, Givebutter, DoubleTheDonation, Brandfetch

---

## 7. RESPONSIVE DESIGN BREAKPOINTS

Using Tailwind CSS breakpoints:
- `sm` - 640px (small devices)
- `md` - 768px (tablets/medium screens)
- `lg` - 1024px (desktops)
- **Max Content Width:** max-w-7xl (80rem)

---

## 8. KEY FILES LOCATION

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/index.html` | Main HTML entry point |
| `/src/index.css` | Global CSS with Tailwind |
| `/tailwind.config.js` | Tailwind theme configuration |
| `/App.tsx` | Main React app component |
| `/components/Footer.tsx` | Footer component (1100px fixed) |
| `/components/Navbar.tsx` | Navigation header |
| `/components/Button.tsx` | Reusable button component |
| `/components/Reveal.tsx` | Scroll animation component |
| `/dist/index.html` | Production build HTML |
| `/dist/assets/css/index-qZtDmf4w.css` | Compiled CSS bundle |

---

## 9. ACCESSIBILITY FEATURES

- Skip-to-main-content link (sr-only, focus visible)
- ARIA labels on navigation
- Minimum 44×44px touch targets for social icons
- Semantic HTML structure
- Color contrast ratios suitable for WCAG compliance
- Focus indicators with ring styling

---

## 10. COPYRIGHT & LEGAL

**Footer Copyright Text:**
```
© 2025 Gifted Dreamers, Inc. All rights reserved.
Gifted Dreamers, Inc. is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.
EIN: 39-3863796 | All donations are tax-deductible.
```

**Links:**
- Privacy Policy: `/privacy`
- Terms of Service: `/terms`
- About: `/about`

---

This codebase demonstrates a modern, performance-optimized React website with strong focus on:
- SEO optimization for nonprofits
- Accessibility compliance
- Performance (CLS prevention, lazy loading, code splitting)
- Brand consistency with custom color palette
- Responsive mobile-first design

**Analytics Events Tracked** (`/lib/analytics.ts`):

## 7. PROJECT STRUCTURE - KEY FILES

```
v3gifteddreamers.git/
├── .github/workflows/
│   ├── deploy.yml          # AWS SCP deployment
│   └── ci.yml              # CI validation
├── components/
│   ├── Navbar.tsx          # Navigation with "Contact Us" CTA
│   ├── Footer.tsx          # Links to Give Back (GRUHP, Matching Gifts)
│   ├── CookieConsent.tsx   # Cookie banner + analytics loader trigger
│   ├── Button.tsx          # Reusable button component
│   ├── OptimizedImage.tsx  # Responsive image component
│   ├── Reveal.tsx          # Scroll animation component
│   ├── LogoCloud.tsx       # Enterprise tools logos
│   ├── ContactForm.tsx     # Contact page form
│   ├── Breadcrumbs.tsx     # Navigation breadcrumbs
│   └── Donate.tsx          # (implied - widget wrapper)
├── pages/
│   ├── Home.tsx            # Hero with $380K value prop
│   ├── Services.tsx        # Tech Perks Audit, Accounting Cleanup
│   ├── MatchingGifts.tsx   # DONATION PAGE: Givebutter + Double the Donation
│   ├── Volunteer.tsx       # DONATION PAGE: Volunteer grants + form to n8n
│   ├── Gruhp.tsx           # DONATION PAGE: GRUHP Fund mutual aid
│   ├── CommonCloud.tsx     # Common Cloud project info
│   ├── Partners.tsx        # Corporate partner programs
│   ├── About.tsx
│   ├── Contact.tsx
│   ├── FAQ.tsx
│   ├── Privacy.tsx
│   └── Terms.tsx
├── lib/
│   ├── analytics-loader.ts # Controls script loading + consent
│   ├── analytics.ts        # GA4 event tracking functions
│   └── image-utils.ts      # Responsive image utilities
├── public/
│   ├── images/             # Hero images, social icons, logos
│   ├── logo-200.png        # Logo
│   └── logos/              # Givebutter, QuickBooks, Airtable icons
├── App.tsx                 # Router + layout (Navbar, Footer, Breadcrumbs)
├── index.tsx               # React entry point
├── index.html              # HTML with structured data + inline critical CSS
├── vite.config.ts          # Build configuration with CSS preload plugin
├── nginx.conf              # Nginx SPA routing config
├── package.json            # Dependencies (React 18, React Router 6, Vite 5)
├── tailwind.config.js      # Tailwind CSS config
├── tsconfig.json           # TypeScript config
├── postcss.config.js       # PostCSS + Tailwind
└── scripts/
    ├── generate-responsive-images.sh
    ├── optimize-images.sh
    └── optimize-pngs.mjs
```

**Dependencies:**
- React 18.2.0
- React Router DOM 6.22.3
- Lucide React (icons)
- Tailwind CSS 4.1.18
- Vite 5.4.0
- TypeScript 5.3.3
- Sharp 0.33.5 (image optimization)
- Terser (minification)

---

## 8. DONATION FLOW SUMMARY

1. **User visits Matching Gifts or GRUHP page**
2. **Page loads Givebutter widget** via `loadGivebutter()` (pages import this function)
3. **Givebutter CDN script loads:**
   - `https://js.givebutter.com/elements/latest.js`
   - `https://widgets.givebutter.com/latest.umd.cjs?acct=PWF9tXFflbTG12rU&p=other`
4. **Widget renders with donation options** (custom buttons via Givebutter account)
5. **User selects amount and donates** → Givebutter processes payment
6. **Analytics tracking** (if user accepted cookies):
   - `trackDonationClick(amount, 'matching-gifts' | 'gruhp')`
   - Event sent to GA4 with conversion category

---

## 9. NO DOCKER CONFIGURATION

The project does **not** use Docker or docker-compose. It's a simple:
- **Build:** Vite produces static HTML/CSS/JS in `dist/`
- **Deploy:** SCP copies `dist/*` to Nginx server
- **Serve:** Nginx serves static files + SPA routing

This is the most efficient approach for a static React app.

---

All file paths use absolute paths as shown. The deployment pipeline is entirely GitHub Actions → AWS Lightsail via SSH key authentication.

**Preconnects:**
- `https://fonts.googleapis.com`
- `https://fonts.gstatic.com`

**Structured Data (JSON-LD):**
- Organization schema with nonprofit details
- EIN: 39-3863796
- Tax ID verified
- Founder: Kristine Socall (Executive Director)
- Links to: Substack, LinkedIn, Givebutter

**GA4 Configuration IDs:**
- G-6X01DRJBC0 (Primary GA4)
- GT-KD7JXRM8 (Secondary config)
- AW-17711415748 (Google Ads conversion)

**Analytics Events Tracked** (`/lib/analytics.ts`):
- `trackDonationClick(amount, location)` - Donation button clicks
- `trackFormSubmission(formType)` - Contact form submissions
- `trackVolunteerSubmission(skills)` - Volunteer signups
- `trackPageView(pagePath, pageTitle)` - Page navigation
- Events sent to GA4 with category "Conversion"

---

## 7. PROJECT STRUCTURE - KEY FILES

```
v3gifteddreamers.git/
├── .github/workflows/
│   ├── deploy.yml          # AWS SCP deployment
│   └── ci.yml              # CI validation
├── components/
│   ├── Navbar.tsx          # Navigation with "Contact Us" CTA
│   ├── Footer.tsx          # Links to Give Back (GRUHP, Matching Gifts)
│   ├── CookieConsent.tsx   # Cookie banner + analytics loader trigger
│   ├── Button.tsx          # Reusable button component
│   ├── OptimizedImage.tsx  # Responsive image component
│   ├── Reveal.tsx          # Scroll animation component
│   ├── LogoCloud.tsx       # Enterprise tools logos
│   ├── ContactForm.tsx     # Contact page form
│   ├── Breadcrumbs.tsx     # Navigation breadcrumbs
│   └── Donate.tsx          # (implied - widget wrapper)
├── pages/
│   ├── Home.tsx            # Hero with $380K value prop
│   ├── Services.tsx        # Tech Perks Audit, Accounting Cleanup
│   ├── MatchingGifts.tsx   # DONATION PAGE: Givebutter + Double the Donation
│   ├── Volunteer.tsx       # DONATION PAGE: Volunteer grants + form to n8n
│   ├── Gruhp.tsx           # DONATION PAGE: GRUHP Fund mutual aid
│   ├── CommonCloud.tsx     # Common Cloud project info
│   ├── Partners.tsx        # Corporate partner programs
│   ├── About.tsx
│   ├── Contact.tsx
│   ├── FAQ.tsx
│   ├── Privacy.tsx
│   └── Terms.tsx
├── lib/
│   ├── analytics-loader.ts # Controls script loading + consent
│   ├── analytics.ts        # GA4 event tracking functions
│   └── image-utils.ts      # Responsive image utilities
├── public/
│   ├── images/             # Hero images, social icons, logos
│   ├── logo-200.png        # Logo
│   └── logos/              # Givebutter, QuickBooks, Airtable icons
├── App.tsx                 # Router + layout (Navbar, Footer, Breadcrumbs)
├── index.tsx               # React entry point
├── index.html              # HTML with structured data + inline critical CSS
├── vite.config.ts          # Build configuration with CSS preload plugin
├── nginx.conf              # Nginx SPA routing config
├── package.json            # Dependencies (React 18, React Router 6, Vite 5)
├── tailwind.config.js      # Tailwind CSS config
├── tsconfig.json           # TypeScript config
├── postcss.config.js       # PostCSS + Tailwind
└── scripts/
    ├── generate-responsive-images.sh
    ├── optimize-images.sh
    └── optimize-pngs.mjs
```

**Dependencies:**
- React 18.2.0
- React Router DOM 6.22.3
- Lucide React (icons)
- Tailwind CSS 4.1.18
- Vite 5.4.0
- TypeScript 5.3.3
- Sharp 0.33.5 (image optimization)
- Terser (minification)

---

## 8. DONATION FLOW SUMMARY

1. **User visits Matching Gifts or GRUHP page**
2. **Page loads Givebutter widget** via `loadGivebutter()` (pages import this function)
3. **Givebutter CDN script loads:**
   - `https://js.givebutter.com/elements/latest.js`
   - `https://widgets.givebutter.com/latest.umd.cjs?acct=PWF9tXFflbTG12rU&p=other`
4. **Widget renders with donation options** (custom buttons via Givebutter account)
5. **User selects amount and donates** → Givebutter processes payment
6. **Analytics tracking** (if user accepted cookies):
   - `trackDonationClick(amount, 'matching-gifts' | 'gruhp')`
   - Event sent to GA4 with conversion category

---

## 9. NO DOCKER CONFIGURATION

The project does **not** use Docker or docker-compose. It's a simple:
- **Build:** Vite produces static HTML/CSS/JS in `dist/`
- **Deploy:** SCP copies `dist/*` to Nginx server
- **Serve:** Nginx serves static files + SPA routing

This is the most efficient approach for a static React app.

---

All file paths use absolute paths as shown. The deployment pipeline is entirely GitHub Actions → AWS Lightsail via SSH key authentication.

Good, I have a thorough understanding of both projects now. Let me also look at the specific deployment files and the Givebutter widget code more closely, and explore the branding colors from the logo.

file.content:
name: Deploy to AWS

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Deploy static files
        uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.AWS_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.AWS_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SSH_KEY }}
          source: "dist/*"
          target: "/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/gifteddreamers.org"
          strip_components: 1

    74→
