GitHub Actions Deployment Workflow File: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` This directly mirrors the v3gifteddreamers workflow from `/Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/v3gifteddreamers.git/.github/workflows/deploy.yml` but is **simpler** because there is no build step: ```yaml name: Deploy to AWS Lightsail on: push: branches: [main] workflow_dispatch: concurrency: group: deploy-${{ github.ref }} cancel-in-progress: true jobs: deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest timeout-minutes: 5 steps: - name: Checkout code uses: actions/checkout@v4 - name: Deploy static files uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7 with: host: ${{ secrets.AWS_HOST }} username: ${{ secrets.AWS_USER }} key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SSH_KEY }} source: "index.html,immigrant-resources.html,kyr-ice-training.html,assets/*,robots.txt,sitemap.xml" target: "/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us" ``` Key differences from v3gifteddreamers: - No `setup-node`, no `npm ci`, no `npm run build` steps. - `source` explicitly lists the files to deploy (not `dist/*`). - Target is `/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us` (the new domain's directory). - `strip_components` is not needed since files are in the root. - Uses the same three GitHub Secrets: `AWS_HOST`, `AWS_USER`, `AWS_SSH_KEY`. **Note**: The GitHub Secrets should already exist on the `Gifted-Dreamers` organization since they're used by v3gifteddreamers. If the justnice.us repo is in the same org, the secrets may need to be shared or duplicated to the new repo. This can be configured in GitHub org settings or set at the repo level. --- ## 6. Nginx Configuration File: `nginx.conf` (stored in repo for reference; deployed manually to server) Based on the v3gifteddreamers pattern from `/Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/v3gifteddreamers.git/nginx.conf`: ```nginx # justnice.us nginx configuration # Static site - no SPA routing needed server { listen 80; server_name justnice.us www.justnice.us; return 301 https://$server_name$request_uri; } server { listen 443 ssl http2; server_name justnice.us www.justnice.us; ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/justnice.us/fullchain.pem; ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/justnice.us/privkey.pem; ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3; ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on; ssl_ciphers ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256; root /home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us; index index.html; # Gzip compression gzip on; gzip_vary on; gzip_min_length 1024; gzip_types text/plain text/css text/xml text/javascript application/javascript application/json application/xml image/svg+xml; # Security headers add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always; add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always; add_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block" always; add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" always; # Cache static assets (logos, favicons) location ~* \.(png|jpg|jpeg|gif|ico|svg|woff|woff2)$ { expires 1y; add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable"; } # Static file serving (NOT SPA routing - each page is its own HTML file) location / { try_files $uri $uri/ =404; } # Deny access to hidden files and markdown source location ~ /\. { deny all; } location ~* \.md$ { deny all; } } ``` **Key differences from v3gifteddreamers nginx.conf**: - `try_files` uses `=404` instead of `/index.html` fallback (this is NOT an SPA) - Added rule to deny access to `.md` source files - Added `image/svg+xml` to gzip types (SVG logos) --- ## 7. Server-Side Setup Tasks (One-Time) These manual steps need to happen on the AWS Lightsail instance: 1. **Create the site directory**: ``` sudo mkdir -p /home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us sudo chown admin:admin /home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us ``` 2. **Set up SSL with Let's Encrypt**: ``` sudo certbot certonly --nginx -d justnice.us -d www.justnice.us ``` 3. **Install the nginx config**: ``` sudo cp nginx.conf /etc/nginx/sites-available/justnice.us sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/justnice.us /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx ``` 4. **DNS Configuration**: Point `justnice.us` and `www.justnice.us` A records to the Lightsail instance IP. This is done at the domain registrar. 5. **GitHub Secrets**: Ensure `AWS_HOST`, `AWS_USER`, `AWS_SSH_KEY` are set on the `Gifted-Dreamers/justnice.us` repo (or available from org-level secrets). --- ## 8. Optional Variant: Markdown Build Pipeline If the user prefers to edit `.md` files and have them auto-converted to HTML, here is the alternative build approach. This adds a `package.json` and build script: ``` justnice.us/ ├── .github/workflows/deploy.yml # Now includes build step ├── src/ │ ├── content/ │ │ ├── immigrant-resources.md │ │ └── kyr-ice-training.md │ └── templates/ │ ├── layout.html # Shared head/header/footer wrapper │ └── content-page.html # Template for content pages ├── assets/ # Same as above ├── build.mjs # Build script (ES module) ├── package.json ├── index.html # Hand-authored landing page (not from MD) ├── nginx.conf └── ... ``` `package.json` would be minimal: ```json { "name": "justnice-us", "private": true, "type": "module", "scripts": { "build": "node build.mjs" }, "devDependencies": { "marked": "^12.0.0" } } ``` `build.mjs` would: 1. Read each `.md` file from `src/content/` 2. Convert to HTML using `marked` 3. Inject into the `layout.html` template (replacing placeholders for title, description, content) 4. Write to `dist/` directory 5. Copy `index.html` and `assets/` to `dist/` The deploy workflow would then match v3gifteddreamers exactly (with `npm ci`, `npm run build`, and `source: "dist/*"`). **Recommendation**: Start with the no-build approach (Section 2-6) for launch speed. Migrate to the build approach later only if frequent markdown editing makes it worthwhile. --- ## 9. Implementation Sequence ### Phase 1: Repository Setup and Core Files 1. Clone the blank repo `Gifted-Dreamers/justnice.us` locally 2. Create `.gitignore` (based on v3gifteddreamers: `.DS_Store`, `node_modules`, `*.local`) 3. Add the asset files (logos, favicons) from `/Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/justNICE.us/` 4. Create `robots.txt` and `sitemap.xml` ### Phase 2: Build the Three HTML Pages 5. Create the shared template components (head, header, footer) -- write them first in `index.html` 6. Build the `index.html` landing page with hero, two-card layout, and quick-reference callout 7. Convert `immigrant-resources.md` to `immigrant-resources.html` -- render all 8 parts plus Quick-Reference and Key Reminders as semantic HTML with the shared template 8. Convert `kyr-ice-training.md` to `kyr-ice-training.html` -- same approach, 4 parts 9. Add the Givebutter widget integration and anonymous contact form to the footer 10. Add the mobile navigation toggle script ### Phase 3: Deployment Pipeline 11. Create `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` 12. Create `nginx.conf` 13. Verify GitHub Secrets on the repo 14. Initial commit and push to `main` to trigger first deployment ### Phase 4: Server Configuration 15. SSH into Lightsail and create the site directory 16. Install the nginx config and obtain SSL certificate 17. Configure DNS for justnice.us 18. Verify the site loads correctly over HTTPS ### Phase 5: Contact Form Webhook 19. Configure n8n webhook workflow (via MCP) that receives form submissions and writes to Airtable 20. Replace `PLACEHOLDER_WEBHOOK_URL` in the HTML with the actual n8n webhook URL 21. Test the form end-to-end --- ## 10. Design Notes ### Color Palette (extracted from logo inspection) - **Primary (maroon/burgundy)**: `#8B1A2B` -- the map pin background color - **Accent (teal/cyan)**: `#0097A7` -- the "JUST NICE" text color - **Bee gold**: `#F5A623` -- hover states, accent highlights - **White**: `#FFFFFF` -- bee circle background, text on dark backgrounds - **Slate-900**: `#0F172A` -- footer background (matches v3gifteddreamers) ### Typography - **Inter** (Google Fonts) -- matches both v3gifteddreamers and socallcgroup-static - Weights: 400 (body), 500 (nav), 600 (sub-headers), 700 (headers) ### Responsive Breakpoints - Mobile-first approach using Tailwind CDN defaults - `md:` (768px) for two-column layouts - `lg:` (1024px) for sidebar TOC on content pages ### Accessibility - All images have descriptive `alt` text - Links have descriptive text (not "click here") - Color contrast: white text on maroon background exceeds WCAG AA (4.5:1 ratio) - Teal on white also passes (approximately 4.6:1) - Phone numbers in hotline tables are rendered as `` for click-to-call on mobile - Tables use `
` headers for screen readers ### Performance - No JavaScript framework -- pure static HTML with CDN-loaded Tailwind - Givebutter scripts load async - Fonts load with `display=swap` to prevent FOIT - PNG logos are already small (25KB for logo, 22KB for favicon) - SVG versions available for further optimization if needed --- ## 11. Potential Challenges 1. **Markdown-to-HTML conversion effort**: The two markdown files total 537 lines with complex tables. Hand-converting the tables to HTML is the most tedious part. Consider using an online markdown-to-HTML converter for the initial conversion, then cleaning up the output. 2. **Emoji rendering in section headers**: The markdown files use emoji (phone, scales, shield, etc.) in section headers. These render fine in modern browsers but should be tested on older systems. Consider adding `aria-hidden="true"` to emoji spans for screen readers. 3. **Link rot**: The markdown files contain 100+ external links. These should be periodically checked. Consider adding a link-checking step to CI (e.g., `lychee` or `linkchecker` as a GitHub Action). 4. **Givebutter widget styling**: The widget has its own styles that may conflict with the maroon/teal color scheme. It loads in an iframe, so conflicts should be minimal, but visual testing is needed. 5. **n8n webhook CORS**: The anonymous contact form submits via `fetch()` from the browser. The n8n webhook endpoint must return proper CORS headers (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://justnice.us`). This is typically handled by n8n's webhook node configuration. 6. **DNS propagation**: After pointing the domain, SSL certificate issuance via Let's Encrypt requires DNS to resolve. Propagation can take up to 48 hours. --- ### Critical Files for Implementation - `/Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/justNICE.us/immigrant-resources.md` - Primary content source (309 lines of markdown to convert to HTML) - `/Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/justNICE.us/kyr-ice-training.md` - Secondary content source (228 lines of markdown to convert to HTML) - `/Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/v3gifteddreamers.git/.github/workflows/deploy.yml` - Reference deployment workflow to adapt (SCP to Lightsail pattern) - `/Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/v3gifteddreamers.git/lib/analytics-loader.ts` - Reference Givebutter integration pattern (lines 53-76: `loadGivebutter()` function) - `/Users/kristinesocall/Library/CloudStorage/ProtonDrive-gifteddreamers@proton.me-folder/2025-12-18 CC/kristinesocall/socallcgroup-static/index.html` - Reference for pure static HTML + Tailwind CDN approach (the exact architectural pattern to follow)
Excellent research. Now let me write the final plan incorporating all requirements, including the Google Maps API for pinning resources and the anonymous contact form.

input.content:
# justNICE.us — Static Site Build & Deploy Plan

## Context

**Problem:** Need a public-facing site at justnice.us to share two comprehensive markdown resource guides (immigrant resources + Know Your Rights training) with communities. The site needs donation integration, an anonymous contact form, and a Google Maps resource pin map.

**Outcome:** A static HTML site deployed to AWS Lightsail via GitHub Actions (same pipeline as gifteddreamers.org), hosted at `justnice.us`.

---

## Architecture: Pure Static HTML (No Build Step)

No React, no Vite, no npm. Three hand-authored HTML files with Tailwind CDN + Inter font. The deploy workflow just SCPs files directly — no build step needed. This matches the simplest possible approach and keeps the repo dependency-free.

---

## Files to Create

```
justnice.us/
├── .github/workflows/deploy.yml     # SCP to Lightsail on push to main
├── assets/
│   ├── Bee-NICE-LOGO.png            # Copied from justNICE.us source dir
│   ├── Bee-NICE-LOGO.svg
│   ├── Bee-NICE-favicon.png
│   └── Bee-NICE-favicon.svg
├── index.html                        # Landing page + Google Maps resource map
├── immigrant-resources.html          # Rendered from immigrant-resources.md
├── kyr-ice-training.html             # Rendered from kyr-ice-training.md
├── immigrant-resources.md            # Source markdown (kept for reference)
├── kyr-ice-training.md               # Source markdown (kept for reference)
├── nginx.conf                        # Server config (reference, deployed manually)
├── robots.txt
├── sitemap.xml
└── .gitignore
```

---

## Branding (from Bee-NICE-LOGO.png)

| Role | Color | Usage |
|------|-------|-------|
| Primary | `#8B1A2B` (deep maroon/burgundy) | Header bg, table headers, hero |
| Accent | `#0097A7` (teal/cyan) | Links, buttons, CTAs |
| Hover/highlight | `#F5A623` (bee gold) | Nav hover, accent highlights |
| Footer bg | `#0F172A` (slate-900) | Footer, dark sections |
| Font | Inter (Google Fonts) | Body + headings |

---

## Page Structure (All 3 Pages)

### Shared `<head>`
- Meta tags (SEO, Open Graph, Twitter Card)
- Favicon: `/assets/Bee-NICE-favicon.png`
- Tailwind CDN with custom color config
- Inter font (Google Fonts, async load)
- DNS prefetch for `js.givebutter.com`, `widgets.givebutter.com`
- Custom CSS for `.content-area` tables, blockquotes, links, lists
- JSON-LD structured data (WebSite schema, publisher: Gifted Dreamers, Inc.)

### Shared Header (sticky, maroon bg)
- Logo (Bee-NICE-LOGO.png) linked to `/`
- Nav links: Immigrant Resources, Know Your Rights, Resource Map, Donate
- Mobile hamburger menu with JS toggle
- "Donate" button (teal bg) linking to `gifteddreamers.org/GRUHP`

### Shared Footer
1. **Givebutter Donate Widget** — dark section with `<givebutter-widget id="pnqxbg">` and text: "All donations go 100% to mutual aid"
2. **Anonymous Contact Form** — textarea only (no name/email/phone), submits to n8n webhook → Airtable. Placeholder URL until webhook is configured via MCP.
3. **Copyright**: `© 2026 Gifted Dreamers, Inc. Texas-based 501(c)(3) EIN 39-3863796` + link to `gifteddreamers.org/GRUHP`

### Givebutter Scripts (bottom of `<body>`)
Copied from v3gifteddreamers `analytics-loader.ts` pattern:
- `window.Givebutter('setOptions', { accountId: 'PWF9tXFflbTG12rU' })`
- Async load `https://js.givebutter.com/elements/latest.js`
- Async load `https://widgets.givebutter.com/latest.umd.cjs?acct=PWF9tXFflbTG12rU&p=other`

---

## Page 1: `index.html` (Landing Page)

1. **Hero**: Maroon gradient bg, Bee-NICE logo, headline "Know Your Rights. Protect Your Community.", two CTA buttons to resource pages
2. **Two-Card Section**: Side-by-side cards linking to each resource page (responsive, stacks on mobile)
3. **Google Maps Resource Map** (see below)
4. **Quick-Reference Callout**: "Key Reminders" from immigrant-resources.md (constitutional rights, don't open door, don't sign, etc.)
5. **Donate CTA**: Teal banner with Givebutter widget
6. **Footer** (shared)

---

## Page 2: `immigrant-resources.html`

Convert the 309-line markdown to semantic HTML:
- Page title banner with last-updated date
- **Sticky sidebar TOC** on desktop (collapsible on mobile) with anchor links to each Part (1–8 + Quick-Reference + Key Reminders)
- Content in `.content-area` div with styled tables, blockquotes, lists
- Phone numbers rendered as `<a href="tel:...">` for click-to-call
- All external links open in new tab (`target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"`)

---

## Page 3: `kyr-ice-training.html`

Convert the 228-line markdown to semantic HTML:
- Same layout pattern as immigrant-resources.html
- S.A.L.U.T.E. table with highlighted acronym letters
- Upcoming training sessions table with date emphasis
- Green checkmarks / red X for CAN/Should NOT sections

---

## Google Maps API Integration

### Purpose
An interactive map on the landing page where users can see pinned resource locations (legal aid offices, rapid response hotlines, mutual aid networks, bond fund orgs, training sessions).

### Implementation
- **API**: Google Maps JavaScript API with Markers
- **API Key**: Will need a Google Maps API key (restricted to `justnice.us` domain). Check if one exists in 1Password or create via Google Cloud Console.
- **Location on page**: Dedicated section on `index.html` below the two-card section, titled "Find Resources Near You"
- **Data source**: A `resources.json` file in the repo root containing an array of pinned locations:
  ```json
  [
    {
      "name": "RAICES (Texas)",
      "type": "legal",
      "lat": 29.4241,
      "lng": -98.4936,
      "address": "San Antonio, TX",
      "phone": "210-XXX-XXXX",
      "url": "https://raicestexas.org",
      "description": "Free legal services, bond fund, asylum representation"
    }
  ]
  ```
- **Pin types** (color-coded markers):
  - Red: Emergency hotlines / rapid response
  - Blue: Legal services
  - Green: Mutual aid networks
  - Purple: Bond funds
  - Orange: Training sessions / events
- **Map features**:
  - Filter buttons by resource type (legal, mutual aid, bond fund, hotlines, training)
  - Info window popups on pin click showing name, description, phone (click-to-call), and website link
  - Cluster markers when zoomed out (using MarkerClusterer library)
  - Default view: continental US
- **File additions**:
  - `resources.json` — resource pin data
  - Google Maps script tag in `index.html` only (not on content pages)

### Files to add to repo structure
```
├── resources.json                    # Map pin data (lat/lng, name, type, etc.)
```

---

## Deployment: GitHub Actions

**File**: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

```yaml
name: Deploy to AWS Lightsail
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:
concurrency:
  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Deploy static files
        uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.AWS_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.AWS_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SSH_KEY }}
          source: "index.html,immigrant-resources.html,kyr-ice-training.html,resources.json,assets/*,robots.txt,sitemap.xml"
          target: "/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us"
```

No build step. Same secrets as v3gifteddreamers (`AWS_HOST`, `AWS_USER`, `AWS_SSH_KEY`). Secrets may need to be duplicated from org-level or set on the new repo.

---

## Nginx Config

Based on v3gifteddreamers `nginx.conf` but simpler (no SPA routing):
- `server_name justnice.us www.justnice.us`
- SSL via Let's Encrypt
- `root /home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us`
- `try_files $uri $uri/ =404` (not SPA fallback)
- Deny access to `.md` files and hidden files
- Gzip + security headers + static asset caching (same as gifteddreamers.org)

---

## Server-Side Setup (One-Time, via SSH)

1. Create directory: `mkdir -p /home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us`
2. SSL cert: `certbot certonly --nginx -d justnice.us -d www.justnice.us`
3. Install nginx config: copy to `/etc/nginx/sites-available/`, symlink to `sites-enabled/`, reload nginx
4. DNS: Point `justnice.us` + `www.justnice.us` A records to Lightsail IP
5. GitHub Secrets: Ensure `AWS_HOST`, `AWS_USER`, `AWS_SSH_KEY` are available on `Gifted-Dreamers/justnice.us` repo

---

## Anonymous Contact Form (n8n → Airtable)

- **Form fields**: Single `<textarea>` — "Paste a URL, share a resource, or send a message..."
- **No PII collected**: No name, email, or phone
- **Submission payload**: `{ message, source: "justnice.us", timestamp }`
- **Webhook**: POST to n8n webhook URL (placeholder until configured via MCP)
- **n8n workflow** (to configure later): Webhook trigger → Write to Airtable
- **UX**: Success/error status message below form, clears on success

---

## Implementation Sequence

| Step | Task | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| 1 | Clone `Gifted-Dreamers/justnice.us` locally | Blank repo |
| 2 | Add `.gitignore`, assets (logos/favicons from source dir) | Copy 4 image files |
| 3 | Create `index.html` with shared template (head, header, footer, Givebutter, contact form) | Landing page with hero, cards, map section |
| 4 | Create `resources.json` with initial resource pins | Seed from organizations in the markdown files |
| 5 | Add Google Maps integration to `index.html` | API key, markers, filters, clustering |
| 6 | Convert `immigrant-resources.md` → `immigrant-resources.html` | 309 lines, 8 parts + appendices |
| 7 | Convert `kyr-ice-training.md` → `kyr-ice-training.html` | 228 lines, 4 parts |
| 8 | Create `robots.txt`, `sitemap.xml` | SEO basics |
| 9 | Create `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` | SCP deployment |
| 10 | Create `nginx.conf` | Reference config |
| 11 | Initial commit + push to trigger deploy | |
| 12 | SSH to Lightsail: create dir, install nginx, SSL | Server setup |
| 13 | Configure DNS for justnice.us | Domain registrar |
| 14 | Configure n8n webhook + Airtable (via MCP) | Replace placeholder URL |
| 15 | Test end-to-end: pages, links, donate widget, contact form, map | |

---

## Verification

- [ ] All 3 pages load over HTTPS at justnice.us
- [ ] Navigation works between all pages (desktop + mobile)
- [ ] Givebutter donate widget renders and accepts donations
- [ ] Anonymous contact form submits successfully (after webhook configured)
- [ ] Google Maps loads with resource pins, filters work, info windows display
- [ ] All external links in resource tables open correctly
- [ ] Phone numbers are click-to-call on mobile
- [ ] Footer shows correct copyright: "© 2026 Gifted Dreamers, Inc. Texas-based 501(c)(3) EIN 39-3863796"
- [ ] Footer links to gifteddreamers.org/GRUHP with "All donations go 100% to mutual aid"
- [ ] Favicon displays correctly
- [ ] Responsive layout works on mobile/tablet/desktop
- [ ] GitHub Actions deploys on push to main

---

## Open Questions

1. **Google Maps API Key**: Do you have an existing Google Maps API key, or should I create one via Google Cloud Console? (Need to check 1Password.)
2. **Domain DNS**: Is justnice.us already registered and do you have access to its DNS settings?
3. **GitHub Secrets**: Are `AWS_HOST`/`AWS_USER`/`AWS_SSH_KEY` already shared at the Gifted-Dreamers org level, or do they need to be added to the new repo?

### Shared Footer
1. **Givebutter Donate Widget** — dark section with `<givebutter-widget id="pnqxbg">` and text: "All donations go 100% to mutual aid"
2. **Anonymous Contact Form** — textarea only (no name/email/phone), submits to n8n webhook → Airtable. Placeholder URL until webhook is configured via MCP.
3. **Copyright**: `© 2026 Gifted Dreamers, Inc. Texas-based 501(c)(3) EIN 39-3863796` + link to `gifteddreamers.org/GRUHP`

### Givebutter Scripts (bottom of `<body>`)
Copied from v3gifteddreamers `analytics-loader.ts` pattern:
- `window.Givebutter('setOptions', { accountId: 'PWF9tXFflbTG12rU' })`
- Async load `https://js.givebutter.com/elements/latest.js`
- Async load `https://widgets.givebutter.com/latest.umd.cjs?acct=PWF9tXFflbTG12rU&p=other`

---

## Page 1: `index.html` (Landing Page)

1. **Hero**: Maroon gradient bg, Bee-NICE logo, headline "Know Your Rights. Protect Your Community.", two CTA buttons to resource pages
2. **Two-Card Section**: Side-by-side cards linking to each resource page (responsive, stacks on mobile)
3. **Google Maps Resource Map** (see below)
4. **Quick-Reference Callout**: "Key Reminders" from immigrant-resources.md (constitutional rights, don't open door, don't sign, etc.)
5. **Donate CTA**: Teal banner with Givebutter widget
6. **Footer** (shared)

---

## Page 2: `immigrant-resources.html`

Convert the 309-line markdown to semantic HTML:
- Page title banner with last-updated date
- **Sticky sidebar TOC** on desktop (collapsible on mobile) with anchor links to each Part (1–8 + Quick-Reference + Key Reminders)
- Content in `.content-area` div with styled tables, blockquotes, lists
- Phone numbers rendered as `<a href="tel:...">` for click-to-call
- All external links open in new tab (`target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"`)

---

## Page 3: `kyr-ice-training.html`

Convert the 228-line markdown to semantic HTML:
- Same layout pattern as immigrant-resources.html
- S.A.L.U.T.E. table with highlighted acronym letters
- Upcoming training sessions table with date emphasis
- Green checkmarks / red X for CAN/Should NOT sections

---

## Google Maps API Integration

### Purpose
An interactive map on the landing page where users can see pinned resource locations (legal aid offices, rapid response hotlines, mutual aid networks, bond fund orgs, training sessions).

### Implementation
- **API**: Google Maps JavaScript API with Markers
- **API Key**: Will need a Google Maps API key (restricted to `justnice.us` domain). Check if one exists in 1Password or create via Google Cloud Console.
- **Location on page**: Dedicated section on `index.html` below the two-card section, titled "Find Resources Near You"
- **Data source**: A `resources.json` file in the repo root containing an array of pinned locations:
  ```json
  [
    {
      "name": "RAICES (Texas)",
      "type": "legal",
      "lat": 29.4241,
      "lng": -98.4936,
      "address": "San Antonio, TX",
      "phone": "210-XXX-XXXX",
      "url": "https://raicestexas.org",
      "description": "Free legal services, bond fund, asylum representation"
    }
  ]
  ```
- **Pin types** (color-coded markers):
  - Red: Emergency hotlines / rapid response
  - Blue: Legal services
  - Green: Mutual aid networks
  - Purple: Bond funds
  - Orange: Training sessions / events
- **Map features**:
  - Filter buttons by resource type (legal, mutual aid, bond fund, hotlines, training)
  - Info window popups on pin click showing name, description, phone (click-to-call), and website link
  - Cluster markers when zoomed out (using MarkerClusterer library)
  - Default view: continental US
- **File additions**:
  - `resources.json` — resource pin data
  - Google Maps script tag in `index.html` only (not on content pages)

### Files to add to repo structure
```
├── resources.json                    # Map pin data (lat/lng, name, type, etc.)
```

---

## Deployment: GitHub Actions

**File**: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

```yaml
name: Deploy to AWS Lightsail
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:
concurrency:
  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Deploy static files
        uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.AWS_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.AWS_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SSH_KEY }}
          source: "index.html,immigrant-resources.html,kyr-ice-training.html,resources.json,assets/*,robots.txt,sitemap.xml"
          target: "/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us"
```

No build step. Same secrets as v3gifteddreamers (`AWS_HOST`, `AWS_USER`, `AWS_SSH_KEY`). Secrets may need to be duplicated from org-level or set on the new repo.

---

## Nginx Config

Based on v3gifteddreamers `nginx.conf` but simpler (no SPA routing):
- `server_name justnice.us www.justnice.us`
- SSL via Let's Encrypt
- `root /home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us`
- `try_files $uri $uri/ =404` (not SPA fallback)
- Deny access to `.md` files and hidden files
- Gzip + security headers + static asset caching (same as gifteddreamers.org)

---

## Server-Side Setup (One-Time, via SSH)

1. Create directory: `mkdir -p /home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us`
2. SSL cert: `certbot certonly --nginx -d justnice.us -d www.justnice.us`
3. Install nginx config: copy to `/etc/nginx/sites-available/`, symlink to `sites-enabled/`, reload nginx
4. DNS: Point `justnice.us` + `www.justnice.us` A records to Lightsail IP
5. GitHub Secrets: Ensure `AWS_HOST`, `AWS_USER`, `AWS_SSH_KEY` are available on `Gifted-Dreamers/justnice.us` repo

---

## Anonymous Contact Form (n8n → Airtable)

- **Form fields**: Single `<textarea>` — "Paste a URL, share a resource, or send a message..."
- **No PII collected**: No name, email, or phone
- **Submission payload**: `{ message, source: "justnice.us", timestamp }`
- **Webhook**: POST to n8n webhook URL (placeholder until configured via MCP)
- **n8n workflow** (to configure later): Webhook trigger → Write to Airtable
- **UX**: Success/error status message below form, clears on success

---

## Implementation Sequence

| Step | Task | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| 1 | Clone `Gifted-Dreamers/justnice.us` locally | Blank repo |
| 2 | Add `.gitignore`, assets (logos/favicons from source dir) | Copy 4 image files |
| 3 | Create `index.html` with shared template (head, header, footer, Givebutter, contact form) | Landing page with hero, cards, map section |
| 4 | Create `resources.json` with initial resource pins | Seed from organizations in the markdown files |
| 5 | Add Google Maps integration to `index.html` | API key, markers, filters, clustering |
| 6 | Convert `immigrant-resources.md` → `immigrant-resources.html` | 309 lines, 8 parts + appendices |
| 7 | Convert `kyr-ice-training.md` → `kyr-ice-training.html` | 228 lines, 4 parts |
| 8 | Create `robots.txt`, `sitemap.xml` | SEO basics |
| 9 | Create `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` | SCP deployment |
| 10 | Create `nginx.conf` | Reference config |
| 11 | Initial commit + push to trigger deploy | |
| 12 | SSH to Lightsail: create dir, install nginx, SSL | Server setup |
| 13 | Configure DNS for justnice.us | Domain registrar |
| 14 | Configure n8n webhook + Airtable (via MCP) | Replace placeholder URL |
| 15 | Test end-to-end: pages, links, donate widget, contact form, map | |

---

## Verification

- [ ] All 3 pages load over HTTPS at justnice.us
- [ ] Navigation works between all pages (desktop + mobile)
- [ ] Givebutter donate widget renders and accepts donations
- [ ] Anonymous contact form submits successfully (after webhook configured)
- [ ] Google Maps loads with resource pins, filters work, info windows display
- [ ] All external links in resource tables open correctly
- [ ] Phone numbers are click-to-call on mobile
- [ ] Footer shows correct copyright: "© 2026 Gifted Dreamers, Inc. Texas-based 501(c)(3) EIN 39-3863796"
- [ ] Footer links to gifteddreamers.org/GRUHP with "All donations go 100% to mutual aid"
- [ ] Favicon displays correctly
- [ ] Responsive layout works on mobile/tablet/desktop
- [ ] GitHub Actions deploys on push to main

---

## Open Questions

1. **Google Maps API Key**: Do you have an existing Google Maps API key, or should I create one via Google Cloud Console? (Need to check 1Password.)
2. **Domain DNS**: Is justnice.us already registered and do you have access to its DNS settings?
3. **GitHub Secrets**: Are `AWS_HOST`/`AWS_USER`/`AWS_SSH_KEY` already shared at the Gifted-Dreamers org level, or do they need to be added to the new repo?

# justNICE.us — Static Site Build & Deploy Plan

## Context

**Problem:** Need a public-facing site at justnice.us to share two comprehensive markdown resource guides (immigrant resources + Know Your Rights training) with communities. The site needs donation integration, an anonymous contact form, and a Google Maps resource pin map.

**Outcome:** A static HTML site deployed to AWS Lightsail via GitHub Actions (same pipeline as gifteddreamers.org), hosted at `justnice.us`.

---

## Architecture: Pure Static HTML (No Build Step)

No React, no Vite, no npm. Three hand-authored HTML files with Tailwind CDN + Inter font. The deploy workflow just SCPs files directly — no build step needed. This matches the simplest possible approach and keeps the repo dependency-free.

---

## Files to Create

```
justnice.us/
├── .github/workflows/deploy.yml     # SCP to Lightsail on push to main
├── assets/
│   ├── Bee-NICE-LOGO.png            # Copied from justNICE.us source dir
│   ├── Bee-NICE-LOGO.svg
│   ├── Bee-NICE-favicon.png
│   └── Bee-NICE-favicon.svg
├── index.html                        # Landing page + Google Maps resource map
├── immigrant-resources.html          # Rendered from immigrant-resources.md
├── kyr-ice-training.html             # Rendered from kyr-ice-training.md
├── immigrant-resources.md            # Source markdown (kept for reference)
├── kyr-ice-training.md               # Source markdown (kept for reference)
├── nginx.conf                        # Server config (reference, deployed manually)
├── robots.txt
├── sitemap.xml
└── .gitignore
```

---

## Branding (from Bee-NICE-LOGO.png)

| Role | Color | Usage |
|------|-------|-------|
| Primary | `#8B1A2B` (deep maroon/burgundy) | Header bg, table headers, hero |
| Accent | `#0097A7` (teal/cyan) | Links, buttons, CTAs |
| Hover/highlight | `#F5A623` (bee gold) | Nav hover, accent highlights |
| Footer bg | `#0F172A` (slate-900) | Footer, dark sections |
| Font | Inter (Google Fonts) | Body + headings |

---

## Page Structure (All 3 Pages)

### Shared `<head>`
- Meta tags (SEO, Open Graph, Twitter Card)
- Favicon: `/assets/Bee-NICE-favicon.png`
- Tailwind CDN with custom color config
- Inter font (Google Fonts, async load)
- DNS prefetch for `js.givebutter.com`, `widgets.givebutter.com`
- Custom CSS for `.content-area` tables, blockquotes, links, lists
- JSON-LD structured data (WebSite schema, publisher: Gifted Dreamers, Inc.)

### Shared Header (sticky, maroon bg)
- Logo (Bee-NICE-LOGO.png) linked to `/`
- Nav links: Immigrant Resources, Know Your Rights, Resource Map, Donate
- Mobile hamburger menu with JS toggle
- "Donate" button (teal bg) linking to `gifteddreamers.org/GRUHP`

### Shared Footer
1. **Givebutter Donate Widget** — dark section with `<givebutter-widget id="pnqxbg">` and text: "All donations go 100% to mutual aid"
2. **Anonymous Contact Form** — textarea only (no name/email/phone), submits to n8n webhook → Airtable. Placeholder URL until webhook is configured via MCP.
3. **Copyright**: `© 2026 Gifted Dreamers, Inc. Texas-based 501(c)(3) EIN 39-3863796` + link to `gifteddreamers.org/GRUHP`

### Givebutter Scripts (bottom of `<body>`)
Copied from v3gifteddreamers `analytics-loader.ts` pattern:
- `window.Givebutter('setOptions', { accountId: 'PWF9tXFflbTG12rU' })`
- Async load `https://js.givebutter.com/elements/latest.js`
- Async load `https://widgets.givebutter.com/latest.umd.cjs?acct=PWF9tXFflbTG12rU&p=other`

---

## Page 1: `index.html` (Landing Page)

1. **Hero**: Maroon gradient bg, Bee-NICE logo, headline "Know Your Rights. Protect Your Community.", two CTA buttons to resource pages
2. **Two-Card Section**: Side-by-side cards linking to each resource page (responsive, stacks on mobile)
3. **Google Maps Resource Map** (see below)
4. **Quick-Reference Callout**: "Key Reminders" from immigrant-resources.md (constitutional rights, don't open door, don't sign, etc.)
5. **Donate CTA**: Teal banner with Givebutter widget
6. **Footer** (shared)

---

## Page 2: `immigrant-resources.html`

Convert the 309-line markdown to semantic HTML:
- Page title banner with last-updated date
- **Sticky sidebar TOC** on desktop (collapsible on mobile) with anchor links to each Part (1–8 + Quick-Reference + Key Reminders)
- Content in `.content-area` div with styled tables, blockquotes, lists
- Phone numbers rendered as `<a href="tel:...">` for click-to-call
- All external links open in new tab (`target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"`)

---

## Page 3: `kyr-ice-training.html`

Convert the 228-line markdown to semantic HTML:
- Same layout pattern as immigrant-resources.html
- S.A.L.U.T.E. table with highlighted acronym letters
- Upcoming training sessions table with date emphasis
- Green checkmarks / red X for CAN/Should NOT sections

---

## Google Maps API Integration

### Purpose
An interactive map on the landing page where users can see pinned resource locations (legal aid offices, rapid response hotlines, mutual aid networks, bond fund orgs, training sessions).

### Implementation
- **API**: Google Maps JavaScript API with Markers
- **API Key**: Will need a Google Maps API key (restricted to `justnice.us` domain). Check if one exists in 1Password or create via Google Cloud Console.
- **Location on page**: Dedicated section on `index.html` below the two-card section, titled "Find Resources Near You"
- **Data source**: A `resources.json` file in the repo root containing an array of pinned locations:
  ```json
  [
    {
      "name": "RAICES (Texas)",
      "type": "legal",
      "lat": 29.4241,
      "lng": -98.4936,
      "address": "San Antonio, TX",
      "phone": "210-XXX-XXXX",
      "url": "https://raicestexas.org",
      "description": "Free legal services, bond fund, asylum representation"
    }
  ]
  ```
- **Pin types** (color-coded markers):
  - Red: Emergency hotlines / rapid response
  - Blue: Legal services
  - Green: Mutual aid networks
  - Purple: Bond funds
  - Orange: Training sessions / events
- **Map features**:
  - Filter buttons by resource type (legal, mutual aid, bond fund, hotlines, training)
  - Info window popups on pin click showing name, description, phone (click-to-call), and website link
  - Cluster markers when zoomed out (using MarkerClusterer library)
  - Default view: continental US
- **File additions**:
  - `resources.json` — resource pin data
  - Google Maps script tag in `index.html` only (not on content pages)

### Files to add to repo structure
```
├── resources.json                    # Map pin data (lat/lng, name, type, etc.)
```

---

## Deployment: GitHub Actions

**File**: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

```yaml
name: Deploy to AWS Lightsail
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:
concurrency:
  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Deploy static files
        uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.AWS_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.AWS_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SSH_KEY }}
          source: "index.html,immigrant-resources.html,kyr-ice-training.html,resources.json,assets/*,robots.txt,sitemap.xml"
          target: "/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us"
```

No build step. Same secrets as v3gifteddreamers (`AWS_HOST`, `AWS_USER`, `AWS_SSH_KEY`). Secrets may need to be duplicated from org-level or set on the new repo.

---

## Nginx Config

Based on v3gifteddreamers `nginx.conf` but simpler (no SPA routing):
- `server_name justnice.us www.justnice.us`
- SSL via Let's Encrypt
- `root /home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us`
- `try_files $uri $uri/ =404` (not SPA fallback)
- Deny access to `.md` files and hidden files
- Gzip + security headers + static asset caching (same as gifteddreamers.org)

---

## Server-Side Setup (One-Time, via SSH)

1. Create directory: `mkdir -p /home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us`
2. SSL cert: `certbot certonly --nginx -d justnice.us -d www.justnice.us`
3. Install nginx config: copy to `/etc/nginx/sites-available/`, symlink to `sites-enabled/`, reload nginx
4. DNS: Point `justnice.us` + `www.justnice.us` A records to Lightsail IP
5. GitHub Secrets: Ensure `AWS_HOST`, `AWS_USER`, `AWS_SSH_KEY` are available on `Gifted-Dreamers/justnice.us` repo

---

## Anonymous Contact Form (n8n → Airtable)

- **Form fields**: Single `<textarea>` — "Paste a URL, share a resource, or send a message..."
- **No PII collected**: No name, email, or phone
- **Submission payload**: `{ message, source: "justnice.us", timestamp }`
- **Webhook**: POST to n8n webhook URL (placeholder until configured via MCP)
- **n8n workflow** (to configure later): Webhook trigger → Write to Airtable
- **UX**: Success/error status message below form, clears on success

---

## Implementation Sequence

| Step | Task | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| 1 | Clone `Gifted-Dreamers/justnice.us` locally | Blank repo |
| 2 | Add `.gitignore`, assets (logos/favicons from source dir) | Copy 4 image files |
| 3 | Create `index.html` with shared template (head, header, footer, Givebutter, contact form) | Landing page with hero, cards, map section |
| 4 | Create `resources.json` with initial resource pins | Seed from organizations in the markdown files |
| 5 | Add Google Maps integration to `index.html` | API key, markers, filters, clustering |
| 6 | Convert `immigrant-resources.md` → `immigrant-resources.html` | 309 lines, 8 parts + appendices |
| 7 | Convert `kyr-ice-training.md` → `kyr-ice-training.html` | 228 lines, 4 parts |
| 8 | Create `robots.txt`, `sitemap.xml` | SEO basics |
| 9 | Create `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` | SCP deployment |
| 10 | Create `nginx.conf` | Reference config |
| 11 | Initial commit + push to trigger deploy | |
| 12 | SSH to Lightsail: create dir, install nginx, SSL | Server setup |
| 13 | Configure DNS for justnice.us | Domain registrar |
| 14 | Configure n8n webhook + Airtable (via MCP) | Replace placeholder URL |
| 15 | Test end-to-end: pages, links, donate widget, contact form, map | |

---

## Verification

- [ ] All 3 pages load over HTTPS at justnice.us
- [ ] Navigation works between all pages (desktop + mobile)
- [ ] Givebutter donate widget renders and accepts donations
- [ ] Anonymous contact form submits successfully (after webhook configured)
- [ ] Google Maps loads with resource pins, filters work, info windows display

## Context

**Problem:** Need a public-facing site at justnice.us to share two comprehensive markdown resource guides (immigrant resources + Know Your Rights training) with communities. The site needs donation integration, an anonymous contact form, and a Google Maps resource pin map.

**Outcome:** A static HTML site deployed to AWS Lightsail via GitHub Actions (same pipeline as gifteddreamers.org), hosted at `justnice.us`.

---

## Architecture: Pure Static HTML (No Build Step)

No React, no Vite, no npm. Three hand-authored HTML files with Tailwind CDN + Inter font. The deploy workflow just SCPs files directly — no build step needed. This matches the simplest possible approach and keeps the repo dependency-free.

## Google Maps API Integration

### Purpose
An interactive map on the landing page where users can see pinned resource locations (legal aid offices, rapid response hotlines, mutual aid networks, bond fund orgs, training sessions).

### Implementation
- **API**: Google Maps JavaScript API with Markers
- **API Key**: Will need a Google Maps API key (restricted to `justnice.us` domain). Check if one exists in 1Password or create via Google Cloud Console.
- **Location on page**: Dedicated section on `index.html` below the two-card section, titled "Find Resources Near You"
- **Data source**: A `resources.json` file in the repo root containing an array of pinned locations:
  ```json
  [
    {
      "name": "RAICES (Texas)",
      "type": "legal",
      "lat": 29.4241,
      "lng": -98.4936,
      "address": "San Antonio, TX",
      "phone": "210-XXX-XXXX",
      "url": "https://raicestexas.org",
      "description": "Free legal services, bond fund, asylum representation"
    }
  ]
  ```
- **Pin types** (color-coded markers):
  - Red: Emergency hotlines / rapid response
  - Blue: Legal services
  - Green: Mutual aid networks
  - Purple: Bond funds
  - Orange: Training sessions / events
- **Map features**:
  - Filter buttons by resource type (legal, mutual aid, bond fund, hotlines, training)
  - Info window popups on pin click showing name, description, phone (click-to-call), and website link
  - Cluster markers when zoomed out (using MarkerClusterer library)
  - Default view: continental US
- **File additions**:
  - `resources.json` — resource pin data
  - Google Maps script tag in `index.html` only (not on content pages)

### Files to add to repo structure

```

## Deployment: GitHub Actions

**File**: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

```yaml
name: Deploy to AWS Lightsail
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:
concurrency:
  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Deploy static files
        uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.AWS_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.AWS_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SSH_KEY }}
          source: "index.html,immigrant-resources.html,kyr-ice-training.html,resources.json,assets/*,robots.txt,sitemap.xml"
          target: "/home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us"
```

No build step. Same secrets as v3gifteddreamers (`AWS_HOST`, `AWS_USER`, `AWS_SSH_KEY`). Secrets may need to be duplicated from org-level or set on the new repo.

---

## Nginx Config

Based on v3gifteddreamers `nginx.conf` but simpler (no SPA routing):
- `server_name justnice.us www.justnice.us`
- SSL via Let's Encrypt
- `root /home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us`
- `try_files $uri $uri/ =404` (not SPA fallback)
- Deny access to `.md` files and hidden files
- Gzip + security headers + static asset caching (same as gifteddreamers.org)

---

## Server-Side Setup (One-Time, via SSH)

1. Create directory: `mkdir -p /home/admin/commoncloud/sites/justnice.us`
2. SSL cert: `certbot certonly --nginx -d justnice.us -d www.justnice.us`
3. Install nginx config: copy to `/etc/nginx/sites-available/`, symlink to `sites-enabled/`, reload nginx
4. DNS: Point `justnice.us` + `www.justnice.us` A records to Lightsail IP
5. GitHub Secrets: Ensure `AWS_HOST`, `AWS_USER`, `AWS_SSH_KEY` are available on `Gifted-Dreamers/justnice.us` repo

---

## Anonymous Contact Form (n8n → Airtable)

- **Form fields**: Single `<textarea>` — "Paste a URL, share a resource, or send a message..."
- **No PII collected**: No name, email, or phone
- **Submission payload**: `{ message, source: "justnice.us", timestamp }`
- **Webhook**: POST to n8n webhook URL (placeholder until configured via MCP)
- **n8n workflow** (to configure later): Webhook trigger → Write to Airtable
- **UX**: Success/error status message below form, clears on success
